---
title: "Figures for the manuscript"
author: "Ziang Zhang"
date: "2026-02-13"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(fastTopics)
library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(scales)
library(scattermore)

data_path <- "../data/"
code_path <- "../code/"
data_path <- "data/"
code_path <- "code/"
source(paste0(code_path, "ROC.R"))
# seurat_meta <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "seurat_meta.rds"))
seurat_meta <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "igt1_96_withtotalvi20260206_clean_ADTonly.Rds"))@meta.data
flashier_snmf_summary <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "flashier_snmf_summary.rds"))
cells_flashier <- rownames(flashier_snmf_summary$L_pm)
cells_seurat <- seurat_meta$cellID
which(!cells_flashier %in% cells_seurat)
L_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$L_pm[cells_flashier %in% cells_seurat, ]

filter_cells_by_total_membership <- function (L, max_val = 10, numiter = 10) {
  n <- nrow(L)
  rows <- 1:n
  for (iter in 1:numiter) {
    x <- rowSums(L)
    cat(sprintf("%d. Filtered out %d cells.\n",iter,sum(x > max_val)))
    i <- which(x <= max_val)
    L <- L[i,]
    rows <- rows[i]
    d <- apply(L,2,max)
    L <- scale_cols(L,1/d)
  }
  return(rows)
}
scale_cols <- function (A, b) t(t(A) * b)
```

Normalize L_pm for better visualization, so max of each column is 1

```{r}
D <- apply(L_pm,2,max)
L_pm_norm <- scale_cols(L_pm,1/D)
F_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$F_pm
F_pm_norm <- scale_cols(F_pm,D)
```

```{r}
# The above normalization absorbs the scale of L into F. We can also normalize L and F separately, so that the max of each column of L and abs(F) is 1, and the scale factor is stored in D.
Dl <- apply(L_pm,2,max)
Df <- apply(abs(F_pm),2,max)
ldf_list <- c()
ldf_list$L_pm_norm <- scale_cols(L_pm,1/Dl)
ldf_list$F_pm_norm <- scale_cols(F_pm,1/Df)
ldf_list$D <- Dl * Df

# check if LDF = LF still holds after normalization, take 100 cells and 100 genes for fast computation
A <- ldf_list$L_pm_norm[1:100,] %*% diag(ldf_list$D) %*% t(ldf_list$F_pm_norm[1:100,])
B <- L_pm[1:100,] %*% t(F_pm[1:100,])
max(abs(A - B)) / max(1, max(abs(B)))
```

```{r}
colnames(L_pm) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(L_pm)))
colnames(F_pm) <- paste0("F", seq_len(ncol(F_pm)))

D <- diag(1 / apply(L_pm, 2, function(x) max(x)))
L <- L_pm %*% D
cells <- filter_cells_by_total_membership(L,numiter = 12)
seurat_meta_filtered <- seurat_meta[cells,]
L_pm_filtered <- L_pm[cells,]
d <- apply(L_pm_filtered,2,max)
L_pm_filtered <- scale_cols(L_pm_filtered,1/d)

# normalize F correspondingly
F_pm_filtered <- scale_cols(F_pm,d)

L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 != "thymocyte", ]
seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes <- seurat_meta_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 != "thymocyte", ]
```

```{r}
d_l <- apply(L_pm_filtered, 2, function(x) max(x))
d_f <- apply(abs(F_pm_filtered), 2, function(x) max(x))
d <- d_l * d_f
hist(d, breaks = 50, main = "Distribution of LDF values", xlab = "LDF")
```


```{r}
# save L_pm_filtered and F_pm_filtered for later use
saveRDS(L_pm_filtered, paste0(data_path, "L_pm_filtered.rds"))
saveRDS(F_pm_filtered, paste0(data_path, "F_pm_filtered.rds"))
```

```{r}
L_pm_filtered <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "L_pm_filtered.rds"))
F_pm_filtered <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "F_pm_filtered.rds"))
seurat_meta_filtered <- seurat_meta[rownames(L_pm_filtered),]
```

```{r}
# also adjust the ldf_list for the filtered data
Dl_filtered <- apply(L_pm_filtered,2,max)
Df_filtered <- apply(abs(F_pm_filtered),2,max)
ldf_list_filtered <- c()
ldf_list_filtered$L_pm_norm <- scale_cols(L_pm_filtered,1/Dl_filtered)
ldf_list_filtered$F_pm_norm <- scale_cols(F_pm_filtered,1/Df_filtered)
ldf_list_filtered$D <- Dl_filtered * Df_filtered

# check if LDF = LF still holds after normalization, take 100 cells and 100 genes for fast computation
A <- ldf_list_filtered$L_pm_norm[1:100,] %*% diag(ldf_list_filtered$D) %*% t(ldf_list_filtered$F_pm_norm[1:100,])
B <- L_pm_filtered[1:100,] %*% t(F_pm_filtered[1:100,])
max(abs(A - B)) / max(1, max(abs(B)))

# save ldf_list_filtered
saveRDS(ldf_list_filtered, paste0(data_path, "ldf_list_filtered.rds"))
```


## Scatterplot of sparsity

```{r}
options(scipen = 999)
pve <- flashier_snmf_summary$pve
eps <- 0 
pve_log <- log10(pve + eps)

pve_log_min <- quantile(pve_log, 0.02, na.rm = TRUE)
pve_log_max <- quantile(pve_log, 0.98, na.rm = TRUE)
pve_log_clipped <- pmin(pmax(pve_log, pve_log_min), pve_log_max)

pve_scaled <- (pve_log_clipped - min(pve_log_clipped)) /
              (max(pve_log_clipped) - min(pve_log_clipped))

n_col <- 100
pal <- colorRampPalette(c("lightgray", "skyblue", "darkblue"))(n_col)
idx_col <- pmin(pmax(1, floor(pve_scaled * (n_col - 1)) + 1), n_col)
col_points <- pal[idx_col]

load(paste0(data_path, "flashier_snmf_fitted_prior.rda"))
l_pi_vec <- c()
for (i in 1:200) {
  l_pi_vec <- c(l_pi_vec, flashier_snmf_fitted_prior$L_ghat[[i]]$pi[1])
}
f_pi_vec <- c()
for (i in 1:200) {
  f_pi_vec <- c(f_pi_vec, flashier_snmf_fitted_prior$F_ghat[[i]]$pi[1])
}

p_cells <- (1 - l_pi_vec)
p_genes <- (1 - f_pi_vec)

pdf("tmp_figures/scatter_e_active_cells_vs_genes.pdf", width = 6, height = 6)
plot(
  p_cells, p_genes,
  xlab = "Expected proportion of active cells",
  ylab = "Expected proportion of active genes",
  log  = "xy",
  col  = col_points,
  pch  = 19,
  xaxt = "n",
  yaxt = "n",
  main = "Expected proportion of active cells vs genes per GP\n(colored by PVE)"
)
ticks <- c(0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 1)
axis(1, at = ticks, labels = paste0(ticks * 100, "%"))
axis(2, at = ticks, labels = paste0(ticks * 100, "%"))
brks_pve <- c(1e-7, 1e-5, 1e-3, 1e-2, 0.1, 0.2)  
brks_pve <- brks_pve[brks_pve >= min(pve) & brks_pve <= max(pve)]
brks_log <- log10(brks_pve + eps)
brks_log_clipped <- pmin(pmax(brks_log, pve_log_min), pve_log_max)
brks_scaled <- (brks_log_clipped - min(pve_log_clipped)) /
               (max(pve_log_clipped) - min(pve_log_clipped))
brks_idx <- pmin(pmax(1, floor(brks_scaled * (n_col - 1)) + 1), n_col)
brks_cols <- pal[brks_idx]
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = format(brks_pve, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2),
  col    = brks_cols,
  pch    = 15,
  pt.cex = 1.5,
  title  = "PVE",
  bty    = "n"
)
dev.off()
```

```{r}
gp_active_cell_counts_level1 <- data.frame(
  Group = seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1,
  Active_Cell_Counts = rowSums(L_pm_filtered > 1e-1)
)

p <- ggplot(gp_active_cell_counts_level1, aes(x = Group, y = Active_Cell_Counts, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.4, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of highly active GPs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
# number of cells in each group
cell_number_group <- table(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1)

# compute number of upper and lower interval of the number of active GP in each group
median_number_group <- gp_active_cell_counts_level1 %>% group_by(Group) %>% summarise(median = median(Active_Cell_Counts)) %>% ungroup()
lower_number_group <- gp_active_cell_counts_level1 %>% group_by(Group) %>% summarise(lower = quantile(Active_Cell_Counts, p = 0.1)) %>% ungroup()
upper_number_group <- gp_active_cell_counts_level1 %>% group_by(Group) %>% summarise(upper = quantile(Active_Cell_Counts, p = 0.9)) %>% ungroup()

# produce a interval plot
# 1. Merge all summary statistics into a single data frame for plotting
plot_data <- list(median_number_group, lower_number_group, upper_number_group) %>%
  reduce(left_join, by = "Group")

# 2. Optional: Add the sample size (n) to the Group labels for better context
# This uses the cell_number_group table you created earlier
counts_df <- as.data.frame(cell_number_group)
colnames(counts_df) <- c("Group", "n")

plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  left_join(counts_df, by = "Group") %>%
  mutate(Group_Label = paste0(Group, "\n(n=", n, ")"))

# 3. Produce the interval plot
# Produce the interval plot sorted by cell count (descending)
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Group_Label, -n), y = median, ymin = lower, ymax = upper)) +
  # Use geom_pointrange to show the median (point) and the 10th-90th interval (lines)
  geom_pointrange(aes(color = Group), size = 0.8) +
  # Customizing the layout
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Active Gene Programs per Group",
    subtitle = "Sorted by cell count (High to Low). Error bars: 10th-90th percentiles.",
    x = "Cell Group (Annotation Level 1)",
    y = "Number of Active GPs"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), # Rotate labels for readability
    legend.position = "none",                         # Remove legend as X-axis identifies groups
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()                # Clean up the background
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Calculate cell counts and create the labels (Group + n)
group_counts <- gp_active_cell_counts_level1 %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  mutate(Group_Label = paste0(Group, "\n(n=", n, ")"))

# 2. Join the labels back to the original data
plot_df <- gp_active_cell_counts_level1 %>%
  left_join(group_counts, by = "Group")

# 3. Create the merged plot
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = reorder(Group_Label, -n), y = Active_Cell_Counts, fill = Group)) +
  # Draw boxplot using the distribution data
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.4, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray40") +
  # Apply your specific color palette
  scale_fill_manual(values = ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1) +
  # Use the descriptive labels from the previous interval plot
  labs(
    title = "Active Gene Programs per Group",
    subtitle = "Sorted by cell count (High to Low).",
    x = "Cell Group (Annotation Level 1)",
    y = "Number of highly active GPs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray30", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  filename = "tmp_figures/boxplot_active_cells_per_GP_level1.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300
)
```




## Structure plot against level 1:

```{r}
set.seed(1234)
color_coding <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1
# create level1_category_to_factor manually now
level1_category_to_factor <- c(
  "Treg" = "K68",
  "CD8" = "K58",
  "nonconv" = "K30",
  "DN" = "K22",
  "CD8aa" = "K29",
  "gdT" = "K3"
)
fit2 <- L_pm_filtered[, level1_category_to_factor, drop = FALSE]
cell_type <- seurat_meta[rownames(fit2), "annotation_level1"]
cells <- which(cell_type == "CD4" | cell_type == "CD8")
cells <- sample(cells,1e5)
cells <- sort(c(cells,which(cell_type != "CD4" & cell_type != "CD8" & cell_type != "thymocyte" & cell_type != "DP")))

structure_plot(fit2[cells,],gap = 40, n = 10000,
               colors = color_coding[names(level1_category_to_factor)],
               grouping = cell_type[cells]) +
  labs(y = "membership",color = "",fill = "") +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1),
    color = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "structure_plot_level1.pdf",
  width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300
)
```

## Heatmap of log2FC of loadings for all the 200 GPs, for each of the level 1 lineage

```{r}
level_1_categories <- unique(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1)

cell_ids <- rownames(L_pm_filtered)
level1 <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1[
  match(cell_ids, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]

reference_median_loading <- matrix(
  NA_real_,
  nrow = length(level_1_categories),
  ncol = ncol(L_pm_filtered),
  dimnames = list(level_1_categories, colnames(L_pm_filtered))
)

for (ct in level_1_categories) {
  other_cells <- which(level1 != ct & !is.na(level1))
  reference_median_loading[ct, ] <- apply(
    L_pm_filtered[other_cells, , drop = FALSE],
    2,
    median,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
}
```


```{r}
# compute median loading for each level-1 lineage and each GP
median_loading_matrix <- sapply(colnames(L_pm_filtered), function(gp) {
  sapply(level_1_categories, function(cat) {
    cells_in_cat <- which(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == cat)
    median(L_pm_filtered[cells_in_cat, gp], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
})
rownames(median_loading_matrix) <- level_1_categories

# also mean
mean_loading_matrix <- sapply(colnames(L_pm_filtered), function(gp) {
  sapply(level_1_categories, function(cat) {
    cells_in_cat <- which(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == cat)
    mean(L_pm_filtered[cells_in_cat, gp], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
})
rownames(mean_loading_matrix) <- level_1_categories
```

```{r}
log2fc_matrix <- log2((median_loading_matrix) / (reference_median_loading))

log2fc_cap <- pmax(pmin(log2fc_matrix, 3), -3)
factor_levels <- colnames(log2fc_cap)
factor_levels <- factor_levels[order(as.numeric(sub("^K", "", factor_levels)))]
level1_names <- rownames(log2fc_cap)

df_long <- as.data.frame(log2fc_cap) |> 
  rownames_to_column("Level1") |> 
  pivot_longer(-Level1, names_to = "GP", values_to = "log2FC") |> 
  mutate(GP = factor(GP, levels = factor_levels),
         Level1 = factor(Level1, levels = level1_names))

gp_anchors <- c("K3","K22", "K29","K30", "K58", "K68")  

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = GP, y = Level1, fill = log2FC)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-3, 3), oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = gp_anchors,
    labels = gp_anchors
  ) +
  labs(x = "Gene Program (GP)", y = "Level 1 Lineage", fill = "Log2 Fold Change") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),  
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "log2FC_heatmap_level1.pdf",
  width = 15, height = 8, dpi = 300
)

```

Also produce a heatmap of the median loading for each level-1 lineage and each GP, with the same order of GPs as the log2FC heatmap:

```{r}
gp_anchors <- c("K3","K22", "K29","K30", "K58", "K68")
  
median_loading_df <- as.data.frame(median_loading_matrix) |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Level1") |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Level1, names_to = "GP", values_to = "MedianLoading") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    GP = factor(GP, levels = factor_levels),
    Level1 = factor(Level1, levels = level1_names)
  )

ggplot(median_loading_df, aes(x = GP, y = Level1, fill = MedianLoading)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "white", high = "#D73027",
    limits = c(0, 0.3),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = gp_anchors,
    labels = gp_anchors
  ) +
  labs(x = "Gene Program (GP)", y = "Level 1 Lineage", fill = "Median Loading") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

For mean

```{r}
mean_loading_df <- as.data.frame(mean_loading_matrix) |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Level1") |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Level1, names_to = "GP", values_to = "MeanLoading") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    GP = factor(GP, levels = factor_levels),
    Level1 = factor(Level1, levels = level1_names)
  )

ggplot(mean_loading_df, aes(x = GP, y = Level1, fill = MeanLoading)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "white", high = "#D73027",
    limits = c(0, 0.3),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = gp_anchors,
    labels = gp_anchors
  ) +
  labs(x = "Gene Program (GP)", y = "Level 1 Lineage", fill = "Mean Loading") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "mean_loading_heatmap_level1.pdf",
  width = 15, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```


## Proportion of cells with high loading in each level-1

```{r}
proportion_high_loading <- sapply(colnames(L_pm_filtered), function(gp) {
  sapply(level_1_categories, function(cat) {
    cells_in_cat <- which(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == cat)
    mean(L_pm_filtered[cells_in_cat, gp] > 0.1, na.rm = TRUE)
  })
})
rownames(proportion_high_loading) <- level_1_categories

# filter out thymocyte, we don't care thymocyte in this plot
proportion_high_loading <- proportion_high_loading[rownames(proportion_high_loading) != "thymocyte", ]
```

Dotplot of proportion of highly active cells for each level-1 lineage and each GP:

```{r}
# only keep GPs that are at least 0.1 in one of the lineages
proportion_high_loading_selected <- proportion_high_loading[, apply(proportion_high_loading, 2, function(x) any(x > 0.1))]

keep_gp <- apply(proportion_high_loading, 2, function(x) any(x > 0.1))
proportion_high_loading_selected <- proportion_high_loading[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]
median_loading_selected <- median_loading_matrix[rownames(proportion_high_loading_selected), keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

clamp <- function(x, lo, hi) pmin(pmax(x, lo), hi)

prop_long <- as.data.frame(proportion_high_loading_selected) |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Level1") |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Level1, names_to="GP", values_to="PropHigh")

med_long <- as.data.frame(median_loading_selected) |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Level1") |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Level1, names_to="GP", values_to="MedianLoading")

proportion_df <- dplyr::left_join(prop_long, med_long, by = c("Level1","GP")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    GP = factor(GP, levels = factor_levels),
    Level1 = factor(Level1, levels = level1_names),
    Prop_clamped = clamp(PropHigh, 0, 0.3)
  )

ggplot(proportion_df, aes(x = GP, y = Level1)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Prop_clamped, color = MedianLoading), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_size(range = c(1, 6), limits = c(0, 0.5)) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D", end = 0.95) +
  labs(
    x = "Gene Program (GP)",
    y = "Level 1 Lineage",
    color = "Median loading",
    size  = "Prop. highly active"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(stringr)

# ==========================================
# 1. Data Selection and Filtering
# ==========================================
# 1) Keep only GPs that are at least 0.1 in one lineage
keep_gp <- apply(proportion_high_loading, 2, function(x) any(x > 0.1, na.rm = TRUE))
mat <- proportion_high_loading[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

# 2) Clamp range for display (0 to 1) to enhance contrast
clamp <- function(x, lo = 0, hi = 1) pmin(pmax(x, lo), hi)
mat_disp <- clamp(mat, 0, 1)

# 3) Drop 'thymocyte' and align rows/columns
level1_names2 <- setdiff(level1_names, "thymocyte")
rows_keep <- intersect(level1_names2, rownames(mat_disp))
cols_keep <- intersect(factor_levels, colnames(mat_disp))

mat_disp <- mat_disp[rows_keep, cols_keep, drop = FALSE]

# ==========================================
# 2. Row Annotation Setup
# ==========================================
# Create row annotation data frame
ann_row <- data.frame(
  Level1 = factor(rows_keep, levels = rows_keep)
)
rownames(ann_row) <- rows_keep

# ==========================================
# 3. Robust Color Mapping (Fixes the Error)
# ==========================================
# Get color library
all_level1_cols <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1

# Extract colors for present levels only
current_levels <- levels(ann_row$Level1)
level1_cols_used <- all_level1_cols[names(all_level1_cols) %in% current_levels]

# Safety Check: If any level in data is missing from library, assign grey
missing_levels <- setdiff(current_levels, names(level1_cols_used))
if (length(missing_levels) > 0) {
  extra_cols <- setNames(rep("grey80", length(missing_levels)), missing_levels)
  level1_cols_used <- c(level1_cols_used, extra_cols)
}

# Final annotation color list
ann_colors <- list(
  Level1 = level1_cols_used
)

# ==========================================
# 4. Plotting the Heatmap
# ==========================================
# Define the heatmap color palette (White to Red)
hm_cols <- colorRampPalette(c("white", "#D73027"))(101)

pheatmap(
  mat_disp,
  color = hm_cols,
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101),
  cluster_rows = FALSE,  # Keep the order of rows_keep
  cluster_cols = FALSE,  # Keep the order of factor_levels
  annotation_row = ann_row,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors,
  border_color = "white", # Light border makes it look cleaner than NA sometimes
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 10,
  fontsize_col = 6,
  angle_col = 90,
  filename = "proportion_high_loading_heatmap_level1.pdf",
  width = 8,
  height = 8,
  main = "Proportion of High Loading Cells per Lineage",
  useRaster = FALSE       # Improves performance for large matrices
)
```




## Plot DGE analysis compare resting versus activation

```{r}
FlashierDGE_corrected <- function (F1, L1, group1, group2, title_plot = ""){
    loadings_group1 = colMeans(L1[group1, ])
    loadings_group2 = colMeans(L1[group2, ])
    loadings_groups = colMeans(L1[c(group1, group2), ])
    mean_genes_group1 = F1 %*% loadings_group1
    mean_genes_group2 = F1 %*% loadings_group2
    mean_genes = F1 %*% colMeans(L1[c(group1, group2), ])
    mean_change_loadings = loadings_group1 - loadings_group2
    fc_genes = F1 %*% mean_change_loadings %>% as.data.frame()

    vplot = data.frame(SYMBOL = names(mean_change_loadings), mean_change_loadings = mean_change_loadings, 
        AveExpr = loadings_groups)
    max_mean_change = ceiling(max(abs(vplot$mean_change_loadings)))
    top_genes = vplot %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(mean_change_loadings))) %>% 
        utils::head(50)
    p1 = ggplot2::ggplot(data = vplot) + ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(x = mean_change_loadings, 
        y = AveExpr), colour = "black", alpha = I(1), size = I(1)) + 
        ggplot2::xlim(-max_mean_change, max_mean_change) + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = top_genes, 
        ggplot2::aes(x = mean_change_loadings, y = AveExpr, label = SYMBOL), 
        size = 3, color = "red", box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.5, 
        segment.color = "grey50", max.overlaps = 20) + ggplot2::theme_minimal() + 
        ggplot2::labs(x = "Difference in Mean Loading", y = "Average Loading", 
            title = title_plot)
    vplot_genes = data.frame(SYMBOL = rownames(fc_genes), log2FC = fc_genes[, 
        1]/log(2), AveExpr = mean_genes[, 1])
    max_fc_genes = ceiling(max(abs(vplot_genes$log2FC)))
    top_genes_genes <- vplot_genes %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(log2FC))) %>% 
        utils::head(50)
    p2 <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = vplot_genes) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(ggplot2::aes(x = log2FC, 
        y = AveExpr), colour = "black", alpha = I(1), size = I(1), 
        pixels = c(512, 512)) + ggplot2::xlim(-max_fc_genes, 
        max_fc_genes) + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = top_genes_genes, 
        ggplot2::aes(x = log2FC, y = AveExpr, label = SYMBOL), 
        size = 3, color = "red", box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.5, 
        segment.color = "grey50", max.overlaps = 20) + ggplot2::theme_minimal() + 
        ggplot2::labs(x = "Fold Change (log2)", y = "Average Expression", 
            title = title_plot)
    list(p1 = p1, p2 = p2, diff_factors = vplot, diff_genes = vplot_genes)
}
```

```{r}
resting_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group == "resting"]
activated_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group == "activated"]
DGE_resting_activation <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                                  L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                                  group1 = activated_cells,
                                                  group2 = resting_cells)

p1 <- DGE_resting_activation$p1 +
  labs(title = "DGE: Activated vs Resting") +
  xlab("Difference in Mean Loading (Activated - Resting)") +
  xlim(c(-0.25, 0.25))

ggsave(p1, filename = "DGE_activated_vs_resting_loading.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

p2 <- DGE_resting_activation$p2 +
  labs(title = "DGE: Activated vs Resting") +
  xlab("(Projected) Log Fold Change in genes (Activated vs Resting)")

ggsave(p2, filename = "DGE_activated_vs_resting_genes.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)
```


## Plot DGE analysis compare resting versus activation

```{r}
# Plot all UMAPs
umap_result <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "umap_result.rds"))
colnames(umap_result) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
umap_result <- umap_result[rownames(L_pm_filtered),]
df_umap <- as.data.frame(umap_result)
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scattermore)
})

set.seed(1)

# 1) move rownames -> cellID
df_umap2 <- df_umap %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("cellID")

# 2) join with metadata by cellID
plot_df <- df_umap2 %>%
  inner_join(
    seurat_meta_filtered %>% select(cellID, annotation_level2_group),
    by = "cellID"
  )

# 3) stratified subsample (keeps rare groups visible)
max_total <- 1000000
min_per_group <- 300
cap_per_group <- 20000

group_sizes <- plot_df %>% count(annotation_level2_group, name = "n")
G <- nrow(group_sizes)
base_per_group <- ceiling(max_total / max(G, 1))

sample_plan <- group_sizes %>%
  mutate(n_take = pmin(n, pmax(min_per_group, pmin(cap_per_group, base_per_group))))

plot_df_sub <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(annotation_level2_group) %>%
  group_modify(~ dplyr::slice_sample(.x, n = sample_plan$n_take[sample_plan$annotation_level2_group == .y$annotation_level2_group])) %>%
  ungroup()

# 4) fast plot
p_umap_level2_group <- ggplot(plot_df_sub, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2)) +
  scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(color = annotation_level2_group), pointsize = 1.2) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2", color = ""
  )

print(p_umap_level2_group)

ggsave(p_umap_level2_group, filename = "UMAP_level2_group.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)
```



## What happen for the two types of gdT with/without K3?

```{r}
gdT_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "gdT"]

gdT_cells_withK3 <- gdT_cells[L_pm_filtered[gdT_cells, "K3"] > 0.1]
gdT_cells_withoutK3 <- gdT_cells[L_pm_filtered[gdT_cells, "K3"] <= 0.1]

table(seurat_meta_filtered$organ_simplified[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% gdT_cells_withK3]) / length(gdT_cells_withK3)
table(seurat_meta_filtered$organ_simplified[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% gdT_cells_withoutK3]) / length(gdT_cells_withoutK3)
```


```{r}
# DGE analysis for gdT with K3 vs without K3
DGE_gdT_K3 <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                      L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                      group1 = gdT_cells_withK3,
                                      group2 = gdT_cells_withoutK3)

p1 <- DGE_gdT_K3$p1 +
  labs(title = "DGE: gdT with K3 vs without K3") +
  xlab("Log Fold Change in loading (gdT with K3 vs without K3)")

ggsave(p1, filename = "DGE_gdT_K3_loading.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

p2 <- DGE_gdT_K3$p2 +
  labs(title = "DGE: gdT with K3 vs without K3") +
  xlab("Log Fold Change in genes (gdT with K3 vs without K3)")

ggsave(p2, filename = "DGE_gdT_K3_genes.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)
```

Compare the gdT with K3 vs without K3 without K29, to see if the difference is driven by K29:

```{r}
gdT_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "gdT"]

gdT_cells_withK3 <- gdT_cells[L_pm_filtered[gdT_cells, "K3"] > 0.1]
gdT_cells_withoutK3withoutK29 <- gdT_cells[L_pm_filtered[gdT_cells, "K3"] <= 0.1 & L_pm_filtered[gdT_cells, "K29"] <= 0.1]

DGE_gdT_K3 <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                      L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                      group1 = gdT_cells_withK3,
                                      group2 = gdT_cells_withoutK3withoutK29)

# which level2 they come from?
table(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% gdT_cells_withK3])/length(gdT_cells_withK3)
table(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% gdT_cells_withoutK3withoutK29])/length(gdT_cells_withoutK3withoutK29)
```




```{r}
gdT_cells_gdT_GKI <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2 %in% c("gdT.G", "gdT.K", "gdT.I")]
gdT_cells_gdT_others <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[(!seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2 %in% c("gdT.G", "gdT.K", "gdT.I")) & seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "gdT"]

# DGE analysis for gdT between gdT.G/K/I vs other gdT
DGE_gdT_K3 <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                      L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                      group1 = gdT_cells_gdT_GKI,
                                      group2 = gdT_cells_gdT_others)

DGE_gdT_K3$p1
```




Create a UMAP for gdT cells:

```{r}
# umap_result <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "umap_result.rds"))
umap_result <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "mde_totalvi2041006.csv"))
colnames(umap_result) <- c("cellID","UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
rownames(umap_result) <- umap_result$cellID
umap_result <- umap_result[, c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")]
umap_result <- umap_result[rownames(L_pm_filtered),]

gdT_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "gdT"]
umap_gdT <- umap_result[gdT_cells,]
df_umap_gdT <- as.data.frame(umap_gdT)
df_umap_gdT$cellID <- rownames(df_umap_gdT)
df_umap_gdT <- df_umap_gdT %>%
  inner_join(
    seurat_meta_filtered %>% select(cellID, annotation_level2),
    by = "cellID"
  )

p_umap_gdT <- ggplot(df_umap_gdT, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2)) +
  scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(color = annotation_level2), pointsize = 1.2) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP of gdT cells",
    x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2", color = ""
  )
```

```{r}
library(Seurat)
gdT_igt1_96_withtotalvi20260206_clean <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Immgen/immgen-t-factors/data/gdT_igt1_96_withtotalvi20260206_clean.Rds")
umap_result_gdT <- Embeddings(gdT_igt1_96_withtotalvi20260206_clean, "mde_incremental")
colnames(umap_result_gdT) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")

gdT_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "gdT"]
umap_gdT <- umap_result_gdT[gdT_cells,]
df_umap_gdT <- as.data.frame(umap_gdT)
df_umap_gdT$cellID <- rownames(df_umap_gdT)
df_umap_gdT <- df_umap_gdT %>%
  inner_join(
    seurat_meta_filtered %>% select(cellID, annotation_level2),
    by = "cellID"
  )

p_umap_gdT <- ggplot(df_umap_gdT, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2)) +
  scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(color = annotation_level2), pointsize = 1.2) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP of gdT cells",
    x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2", color = ""
  )
p_umap_gdT

ggsave(p_umap_gdT, filename = "UMAP_gdT_level2.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)
```

Plot the loading of K3 and K29 on the UMAP of gdT cells:

```{r}
plot_loadings_on_umap_v2 <- function(umap, loading, factor_num,
                                     size = 0.25,
                                     show_bg = TRUE,
                                     bg_alpha = 0.05,
                                     bg_color = "grey90",
                                     clip_q = 0.99,
                                     top_q = NULL,
                                     viridis_option = "viridis",
                                     viridis_direction = 1) {
  stopifnot(is.matrix(umap) || is.data.frame(umap))
  df <- as.data.frame(umap)
  stopifnot(all(c("UMAP_1","UMAP_2") %in% colnames(df)))
  stopifnot(nrow(df) == length(loading))

  q_hi <- stats::quantile(loading, probs = clip_q, na.rm = TRUE)
  loading_clip <- pmin(loading, q_hi)

  if (!is.null(top_q)) {
    stopifnot(top_q > 0 && top_q < 1)
    thr <- stats::quantile(loading_clip, probs = top_q, na.rm = TRUE)
    loading_plot <- ifelse(loading_clip >= thr, loading_clip, NA_real_)
  } else {
    loading_plot <- loading_clip
  }

  df$loading_plot <- loading_plot

  p <- ggplot() +
    # --- background layer (raster) ---
    {
      if (show_bg)
        scattermore::geom_scattermore(
          data = df,
          mapping = aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2),
          pointsize = size,          # size in pixels
          color = bg_color,
          alpha = bg_alpha
        )
    } +
    # --- colored loading layer (raster) ---
    scattermore::geom_scattermore(
      data = df,
      mapping = aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, color = loading_plot),
      pointsize = size*2,
      alpha = 1,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(
      option   = viridis_option,
      direction = viridis_direction,
      limits   = c(0, q_hi),
      oob      = scales::squish,
      breaks   = scales::pretty_breaks(4),
      name     = paste0("K", factor_num, " loading")
    ) +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Factor ", factor_num),
      x = "", y = ""
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

  p
}
```


```{r}
df_umap_gdT$K3_loading <- L_pm_filtered[df_umap_gdT$cellID, "K3"]
df_umap_gdT$K29_loading <- L_pm_filtered[df_umap_gdT$cellID, "K29"]
df_umap_gdT$K6_loading <- L_pm_filtered[df_umap_gdT$cellID, "K6"]

p_umap_gdT_K3_v2 <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(umap = df_umap_gdT[, c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")],
                                              loading = df_umap_gdT$K3_loading,
                                              factor_num = 3,
                                              size = 0.6,
                                              show_bg = TRUE,
                                              bg_alpha = 0.1,
                                              bg_color = "grey90",
                                              clip_q = 0.99,
                                              top_q = NULL,
                                              viridis_option = "viridis",
                                              viridis_direction = 1)
p_umap_gdT_K29_v2 <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(umap = df_umap_gdT[, c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")],
                                               loading = df_umap_gdT$K29_loading,
                                               factor_num = 29,
                                               size = 0.6,
                                               show_bg = TRUE,
                                               bg_alpha = 0.1,
                                               bg_color = "grey90",
                                               clip_q = 0.99,
                                               top_q = NULL,
                                               viridis_option = "viridis",
                                               viridis_direction = 1)



p_umap_gdT_K6_v2 <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(umap = df_umap_gdT[, c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")],
                                              loading = df_umap_gdT$K6_loading,
                                              factor_num = 6,
                                              size = 0.6,
                                              show_bg = TRUE,
                                              bg_alpha = 0.1,
                                              bg_color = "grey90",
                                              clip_q = 0.99,
                                              top_q = NULL,
                                              viridis_option = "viridis",
                                              viridis_direction = 1)


ggsave(p_umap_gdT_K3_v2, filename = "UMAP_gdT_K3_loading_v2.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

ggsave(p_umap_gdT_K29_v2, filename = "UMAP_gdT_K29_loading_v2.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)

ggsave(p_umap_gdT_K6_v2, filename = "UMAP_gdT_K6_loading_v2.pdf", width = 5, height = 4)
```

## Compare DGE in CD4 and CD8 when comparing activated vs resting

```{r}
# DGE of CD4 activated vs resting
CD4_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "CD4"]
CD4_resting_cells <- CD4_cells[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[match(CD4_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)] == "resting"]
CD4_activated_cells <- CD4_cells[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[match(CD4_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)] == "activated"]

CD4_DGE <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                      L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                      group1 = CD4_activated_cells,
                                      group2 = CD4_resting_cells)

# DGE of CD8 activated vs resting
CD8_cells <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == "CD8"]
CD8_resting_cells <- CD8_cells[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[match(CD8_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)] == "resting"]
CD8_activated_cells <- CD8_cells[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[match(CD8_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)] == "activated"]

CD8_DGE <- FlashierDGE_corrected(F1 = F_pm_filtered,
                                      L1 = L_pm_filtered,
                                      group1 = CD8_activated_cells,
                                      group2 = CD8_resting_cells)
```

```{r}
top_quadrant_labels <- function(df, x, y, label, n = 10) {
  x <- rlang::ensym(x)
  y <- rlang::ensym(y)
  label <- rlang::ensym(label)

  df %>%
    mutate(
      quad = case_when(
        !!x >= 0 & !!y >= 0 ~ "++",
        !!x <  0 & !!y <  0 ~ "--",
        !!x >= 0 & !!y <  0 ~ "+-",
        TRUE                ~ "-+"
      ),
      score = case_when(
        quad == "++" ~  (!!x) + (!!y),
        quad == "--" ~ - (!!x) - (!!y),
        quad == "+-" ~  (!!x) - (!!y),
        quad == "-+" ~ - (!!x) + (!!y)
      ),
      lab_col = recode(
        quad,
        "++" = "darkgreen",
        "--" = "darkred",
        "+-" = "darkblue",
        "-+" = "purple"
      )
    ) %>%
    group_by(quad) %>%
    slice_max(score, n = n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup()
}
```

```{r}
# merge the DGE results for GPs:CD8_DGE$diff_factors and CD4_DGE$diff_factors
diff_factors_CD4 <- CD4_DGE$diff_factors %>%
  rename(mean_change_loadings_CD4 = mean_change_loadings,
         AveExpr_CD4 = AveExpr)

diff_factors_CD8 <- CD8_DGE$diff_factors %>%
  rename(mean_change_loadings_CD8 = mean_change_loadings,
         AveExpr_CD8 = AveExpr)

diff_factors_merged <- diff_factors_CD4 %>%
  inner_join(diff_factors_CD8, by = "SYMBOL")

label_df <- top_quadrant_labels(diff_factors_merged, mean_change_loadings_CD4, mean_change_loadings_CD8, SYMBOL, n = 200)

ggplot(diff_factors_merged, aes(mean_change_loadings_CD4, mean_change_loadings_CD8)) +
  geom_point() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.1, 0.2), ylim = c(-0.2, 0.2)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = label_df,
    aes(label = SYMBOL, color = lab_col),
    max.overlaps = 100,
    size = 3, box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.5, segment.color = "grey50",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(
    x = "Difference in Mean Loading (CD4 Activated vs Resting)",
    y = "Difference in Mean Loading (CD8 Activated vs Resting)",
    # x = "Rel Difference in Mean Loading (CD4 Activated vs Resting)",
    # y = "Rel Difference in Mean Loading (CD8 Activated vs Resting)",
    title = "Comparison of GP Loadings between CD4 and CD8"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_loadings.pdf", 
       width = 7, height = 6)

```

```{r}
# similar plot for genes
diff_genes_CD4 <- CD4_DGE$diff_genes %>%
  rename(log2FC_CD4 = log2FC,
         AveExpr_CD4 = AveExpr)
diff_genes_CD8 <- CD8_DGE$diff_genes %>%
  rename(log2FC_CD8 = log2FC,
         AveExpr_CD8 = AveExpr)

diff_genes_merged <- diff_genes_CD4 %>%
  inner_join(diff_genes_CD8, by = "SYMBOL")

diff_genes_merged <- CD4_DGE$diff_genes %>%
  transmute(SYMBOL, log2FC_CD4 = log2FC, AveExpr_CD4 = AveExpr) %>%
  inner_join(
    CD8_DGE$diff_genes %>% transmute(SYMBOL, log2FC_CD8 = log2FC, AveExpr_CD8 = AveExpr),
    by = "SYMBOL"
  )

label_df <- top_quadrant_labels(diff_genes_merged, log2FC_CD4, log2FC_CD8, SYMBOL, n = 10)

ggplot(diff_genes_merged, aes(log2FC_CD4, log2FC_CD8)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = label_df,
    aes(label = SYMBOL, color = lab_col),
    size = 3, box.padding = 0.10, point.padding = 0.15, segment.color = "grey50",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(
    x = "Log2 Fold Change (CD4 Activated vs Resting)",
    y = "Log2 Fold Change (CD8 Activated vs Resting)",
    title = "Comparison of Gene Fold Changes between CD4 and CD8"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_genes.pdf", 
       width = 7, height = 6)
```

## Heatmap for the selected GPs and their top associated genes:

```{r}
# Examples of both up regulated: K25, K26
# Examples of only up regulated in CD8: K10, K43
# Examples of only up regulated in CD4: K12, K58
# Examples of down regulated in both: K171, K9
# GPs_of_interest <- c("K25", "K26", "K10", "K43", "K12", "K58", "K171", "K9")
GPs_of_interest <- paste0("K", c(25, 26, 10, 43, 12, 58, 171, 9, 79, 35, 72, 177, 161, 152, 162, 36, 56))
kset <- GPs_of_interest

F_pm_filtered_norm <- scale_cols(F_pm_filtered, 1 / apply(abs(F_pm_filtered), 2, max))
colnames(F_pm_filtered_norm) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(F_pm_filtered_norm)))

F_pm_filtered_norm_subset <- F_pm_filtered_norm[, GPs_of_interest, drop = FALSE]

jj <- annotation_heatmap(F_pm_filtered_norm_subset,n = 3,
                   dim = kset,
                   show_dims = kset,
                   compare_dims = kset,
                   select_features = "largest",
                   feature_sign = "both",
                   zero_value = 0.05,
                   font_size = 15) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "plain",size = 10))
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(tibble)

df <- jj@data %>%
  transmute(gene = feature_name, gp = dim, value = as.numeric(value))

# gene x gp matrix
mat <- df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = gp, values_from = value, values_fill = 0) %>%  # <- fill missing as 0
  column_to_rownames("gene") %>%
  as.matrix()

# optional: drop genes that are all zeros
mat <- mat[rowSums(abs(mat)) > 0, , drop = FALSE]

# diverging colors: blue (neg) - white (0) - red (pos)
max_abs <- max(abs(mat), na.rm = TRUE)
bk <- seq(-max_abs, max_abs, length.out = 101)
cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#4575B4", "white", "#D73027"))(100)

# reorder the column
mat <- mat[, kset, drop = FALSE]

pheatmap(
  mat,
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  # border_color = NA,
  filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_GPs_heatmap.pdf", # save
  width = 6,
  height = 8
)
```



## Plots showing the lineage-specific and lineage-shared GPs:

```{r}
compute_lineage_proportion <- function(lineage){
  L_pm_filtered_lineage <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == lineage, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # count proportion of cells in each GP with loading >= 0.1
  lineage_proportion <- apply(L_pm_filtered_lineage, 2, function(x) mean(x >= 0.1, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  lineage_proportion
}
compute_lineage_median <- function(lineage){
  L_pm_filtered_lineage <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == lineage, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # compute median loading of each GP in this lineage
  lineage_median <- apply(L_pm_filtered_lineage, 2, function(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  lineage_median
}

# do this for the 7 lineages of interest:
lineages <- c("CD4", "CD8", "Treg", "DN", "Tz", "CD8aa", "gdT")
lineage_proportions <- lapply(lineages, compute_lineage_proportion)
lineage_medians <- lapply(lineages, compute_lineage_median)

# combine into a matrix
lineage_proportions_mat <- do.call(rbind, lineage_proportions)
lineage_medians_mat <- do.call(rbind, lineage_medians)
rownames(lineage_proportions_mat) <- lineages
rownames(lineage_medians_mat) <- lineages
```


Heatmap:

```{r}
# make a heatmap of lineage_proportions_mat
library(pheatmap)
lineage_proportions_mat_sparse <- lineage_proportions_mat

# only keep GPs that appear in at least one lineage with proportion >= 0.01
keep_gps <- colSums(lineage_proportions_mat_sparse >= 0.01, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
lineage_proportions_mat_sparse <- lineage_proportions_mat_sparse[, keep_gps, drop = FALSE]

heatmap_lineage_active <- pheatmap(lineage_proportions_mat_sparse,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "red"))(100),
         breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101),
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         show_colnames = TRUE,
         border_color = "grey",
         filename = "gp_lineage_proportions_heatmap.pdf",
         width = 10,
         height = 6)
```

Dotplot:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
plot_gp_lineage_dotplot <- function(lineage_proportions_mat, 
                                    lineage_medians_mat,
                                    percent_threshold = 0.05,
                                    min_lineages = 1,
                                    max_gps = 200,
                                    log_size = FALSE,
                                    border_color = "grey70",
                                    lineage_order = NULL, 
                                    gp_order = NULL) {    # New: vector of GP names
  
  # 1. Convert Proportions and Medians to long format
  df_prop <- as.data.frame(as.table(as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)))
  colnames(df_prop) <- c("Lineage", "GP", "Proportion")
  
  df_med <- as.data.frame(as.table(as.matrix(lineage_medians_mat)))
  colnames(df_med) <- c("Lineage", "GP", "MedianLoading")
  
  # 2. Merge data
  df_plot <- left_join(df_prop, df_med, by = c("Lineage", "GP"))
  
  # 3. Filter: Keep GPs that pass percent_threshold in >= min_lineages
  keep_gps <- df_plot %>%
    group_by(GP) %>%
    summarize(n_active = sum(Proportion >= percent_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    filter(n_active >= min_lineages) %>%
    pull(GP)
  
  if (length(keep_gps) == 0) {
    stop(paste("No GPs pass the threshold in at least", min_lineages, "lineages."))
  }
  
  df_plot <- df_plot %>% filter(GP %in% keep_gps)
  
  # 4. Limit number of GPs for readability
  if (length(unique(df_plot$GP)) > max_gps) {
    top_gps <- df_plot %>%
      group_by(GP) %>%
      summarize(max_p = max(Proportion, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      arrange(desc(max_p)) %>%
      head(max_gps) %>%
      pull(GP)
    df_plot <- df_plot %>% filter(GP %in% top_gps)
  }

  # 5. Handle Manual Ordering
  # Apply lineage order (Y-axis)
  if (!is.null(lineage_order)) {
    # Intersect with existing names to avoid errors if a lineage was filtered out
    existing_lineages <- intersect(lineage_order, unique(df_plot$Lineage))
    df_plot$Lineage <- factor(df_plot$Lineage, levels = rev(existing_lineages)) 
    # Note: rev() used because ggplot plots from bottom-to-top
  }
  
  # Apply GP order (X-axis)
  if (!is.null(gp_order)) {
    existing_gps <- intersect(gp_order, unique(df_plot$GP))
    df_plot$GP <- factor(df_plot$GP, levels = existing_gps)
  }

  # 6. Handle Transformation
  if (log_size) {
    df_plot$size_val <- log10(df_plot$Proportion * 100 + 1)
    size_label <- "log10(Prop% + 1)"
  } else {
    df_plot$size_val <- df_plot$Proportion
    size_label <- "Proportion"
  }

  # 7. Build the Dotplot
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = GP, y = Lineage)) +
    geom_point(aes(size = size_val, fill = MedianLoading), 
               shape = 21, color = border_color, stroke = 0.2) +
    
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, name = "Median\nLoading") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(0, 6), name = size_label) +
    
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey95"),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "GP Lineage Dotplot",
      subtitle = paste0("Showing GPs active in \u2265 ", min_lineages, " lineages",
                        if(log_size) " (Log-scaled size)" else ""),
      x = "Gene Programs",
      y = "Cell Lineages"
    )

  return(p)
}

# obtain clustering
heatmap_lineage_active_all <- pheatmap(lineage_proportions_mat,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "red"))(100),
         breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101),
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         show_colnames = TRUE,
         border_color = "grey",
         width = 10,
         height = 6)

# Usage:
p_dot <- plot_gp_lineage_dotplot(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                 lineage_medians_mat,
                                 log_size = TRUE,
                                 border_color = "black",
                                 lineage_order = sort(lineages),
                                 gp_order = colnames(lineage_proportions_mat)[heatmap_lineage_active_all$tree_col$order], 
                                 min_lineages = 2)
ggsave(p_dot, filename = "gp_lineage_dotplot_log.pdf", width = 11, height = 7)

p_dot <- plot_gp_lineage_dotplot(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                 lineage_medians_mat,
                                 log_size = FALSE,
                                 border_color = "black",
                                 lineage_order = sort(lineages),
                                 gp_order = colnames(lineage_proportions_mat)[heatmap_lineage_active_all$tree_col$order], 
                                 min_lineages = 2)
ggsave(p_dot, filename = "gp_lineage_dotplot.pdf", width = 11, height = 7)
```



Produce a table:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

tabulate_gp_shared_occupancy <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                         threshold_vec = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.3),
                                         min_lineages_vec = c(1, 2, 3)) {
  
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  n_total_gps <- ncol(mat)
  
  # Helper to calculate counts for a single threshold
  get_counts <- function(t) {
    # Count how many lineages each GP appears in at threshold t
    appear_counts <- colSums(mat >= t, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Calculate counts for each 'at least X lineages' requirement
    counts <- sapply(min_lineages_vec, function(m) sum(appear_counts >= m))
    names(counts) <- paste0("Shared_\u2265", min_lineages_vec)
    
    # Add metadata
    return(c(Threshold = t, Total_GPs = n_total_gps, counts))
  }
  
  # Run for all thresholds and convert to a clean data frame
  res_list <- lapply(threshold_vec, get_counts)
  res_df <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, res_list))
  
  # Add a percentage column for the first shared requirement (usually >= 2)
  # to show how much of the library you are retaining
  if (ncol(res_df) > 3) {
    main_col <- names(res_df)[4] # Usually Shared_>=2
    res_df$Percent_Retained <- round((res_df[[main_col]] / n_total_gps) * 100, 1)
  }
  
  return(res_df)
}

# Generate the table
thresh_table <- tabulate_gp_shared_occupancy(
  lineage_proportions_mat,
  threshold_vec = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.5),
  min_lineages_vec = c(2:7) # Specific sharedness levels you care about
)

# View the table
print(thresh_table)
```



Scatter plot showing the threshold and number of GPs that are shared (>=2 lineages)

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)

plot_gp_shared_occupancy <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                     min_lineages = 2,
                                     threshold_range = seq(0, 0.5, by = 0.005),
                                     highlight_threshold = NULL, # New argument
                                     loading_fixed = 0.1,
                                     base_size = 12,
                                     log_y = FALSE) {
  
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  
  # 1. Calculate occupancy for each threshold in the range
  df_shared <- data.frame(
    threshold = threshold_range,
    count = sapply(threshold_range, function(t) {
      appear_counts <- colSums(mat >= t, na.rm = TRUE)
      sum(appear_counts >= min_lineages)
    })
  )

  # 2. Build the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_shared, aes(x = threshold, y = count)) +
    geom_line(color = "firebrick", linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_area(fill = "firebrick", alpha = 0.1) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Shared GP Occupancy (\u2265 ", min_lineages, " Lineages)"),
      subtitle = paste0("Fixed Loading Thresh: ", loading_fixed),
      x = "Proportion Threshold (percent_threshold)",
      y = paste("Number of GPs shared across \u2265", min_lineages, "lineages")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  # 3. Handle Highlight Threshold
  if (!is.null(highlight_threshold)) {
    # Calculate count at the specific highlight point
    h_count <- sum(colSums(mat >= highlight_threshold, na.rm = TRUE) >= min_lineages)
    
    p <- p + 
      geom_vline(xintercept = highlight_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
      # Add a point at the intersection
      geom_point(aes(x = highlight_threshold, y = h_count), color = "black", size = 3) +
      # Add the label
      annotate("text", x = highlight_threshold, y = h_count, 
               label = paste0("Thresh: ", highlight_threshold, "\nGPs: ", h_count),
               vjust = -1, hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = (base_size/3))
  }

  # 4. Handle Log Scale
  if (log_y) {
    p <- p + scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_log(), breaks = scales::breaks_log())
  } else {
    p <- p + expand_limits(y = 0)
  }

  return(p)
}

# See how many GPs are shared by at least 2 lineages from 0% to 40% threshold
p_occupancy <- plot_gp_shared_occupancy(
  lineage_proportions_mat, 
  min_lineages = 2, 
  highlight_threshold = 0.1,
  threshold_range = seq(0, 0.4, by = 0.002),
  log_y = FALSE
)

print(p_occupancy)
ggsave(filename = "gp_shared_occupancy.pdf", plot = p_occupancy, width = 6, height = 4)
```


Barplot:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
plot_gp_lineage_hist <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                 percent_threshold = 0.1,
                                 loading_threshold = 0.1,
                                 lineages = NULL,
                                 main = NULL,
                                 show_counts = TRUE) {
  
  # Validation and Matrix Conversion
  stopifnot(is.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat) || is.data.frame(lineage_proportions_mat))
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  n_lineages <- nrow(mat)
  
  # Calculate how many lineages each GP appears in
  appear_counts <- colSums(mat >= percent_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Create a frequency data frame for plotting
  df_plot <- as.data.frame(table(factor(appear_counts, levels = 0:n_lineages)))
  colnames(df_plot) <- c("n_lineages_count", "freq")
  df_plot$n_lineages_count <- as.numeric(as.character(df_plot$n_lineages_count))

  # Default Title
  if (is.null(main)) {
    main <- paste0("GPs appearing across lineages\n", 
                   "(Prop \u2265 ", percent_threshold, "; Loading \u2265 ", loading_threshold, ")")
  }

  # Build the ggplot
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = n_lineages_count, y = freq)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n_lineages) +
    labs(
      title = main,
      x = sprintf("Number of lineages (out of %d)", n_lineages),
      y = "Number of GPs"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  # Add text labels if requested
  if (show_counts) {
    p <- p + geom_text(aes(label = freq), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
             expand_limits(y = max(df_plot$freq) * 1.1) # Auto-adds headroom
  }

  print(p)

  # Return data invisibly like the original
  invisible(list(
    freq = df_plot$freq,
    appear_counts = appear_counts,
    breaks = df_plot$n_lineages_count
  ))
}

res <- plot_gp_lineage_hist(lineage_proportions_mat,
                            percent_threshold = 0.1,
                            loading_threshold = 0.1)

ggsave(filename = "gp_lineage_histogram.pdf", plot = last_plot(), width = 6, height = 4)
```


Stacked barplot:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

plot_gp_percent_sensitivity <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                        percent_vec = c(0.1, 0.15, 0.2),
                                        loading_fixed = 0.1,
                                        base_size = 12) {
  
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  n_lineages <- nrow(mat)
  
  # Helper to generate frequency data for each percent threshold
  df_p <- do.call(rbind, lapply(percent_vec, function(p) {
    # Count how many lineages pass the threshold 'p' for each GP
    appear_counts <- colSums(mat >= p, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Create frequency table for 0 to n_lineages
    df <- as.data.frame(table(factor(appear_counts, levels = 0:n_lineages)))
    colnames(df) <- c("n_shared", "count")
    df$n_shared <- as.numeric(as.character(df$n_shared))
    df$p_val <- p
    return(df)
  }))

  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(df_p, aes(x = n_shared, y = count, fill = factor(p_val))) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"), alpha = 0.8, color = "white") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n_lineages) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", name = "Percent Thresh") +
    labs(
      title = paste0("GP Sharedness Sensitivity (Loading Fixed at ", loading_fixed, ")"),
      x = sprintf("Number of Lineages (out of %d)", n_lineages),
      y = "Number of GPs"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  return(p)
}

# Example call:
p_sens <- plot_gp_percent_sensitivity(lineage_proportions_mat, 
                                      percent_vec = c(1e-3, 0.01, 0.1, 0.15, 0.5),
                                      loading_fixed = 0.1)
print(p_sens)

# Save the figure
ggsave("gp_threshold_comparison.pdf", plot = p_sens, width = 8, height = 8)
```

Do a similar plot showing the lines:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales) # Required for nice log labels

plot_gp_percent_line_sensitivity <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                             percent_vec = c(0.1, 0.15, 0.2),
                                             loading_fixed = 0.1,
                                             base_size = 12,
                                             log_y = FALSE) {
  
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  n_lineages <- nrow(mat)
  
  # Generate frequency data
  df_p <- do.call(rbind, lapply(percent_vec, function(p) {
    appear_counts <- colSums(mat >= p, na.rm = TRUE)
    df <- as.data.frame(table(factor(appear_counts, levels = 0:n_lineages)))
    colnames(df) <- c("n_shared", "count")
    df$n_shared <- as.numeric(as.character(df$n_shared))
    df$p_val <- factor(p)
    return(df)
  }))

  # Create the Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_p, aes(x = n_shared, y = count, color = p_val, linetype = p_val, group = p_val)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n_lineages) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "viridis", name = "Percent Thresh") +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = rep(c("solid", "dashed", "dotted", "dotdash"), length.out = length(percent_vec)),
                          name = "Percent Thresh") +
    labs(
      title = paste0("GP Sharedness Trend (Loading Fixed at ", loading_fixed, ")"),
      x = sprintf("Number of Lineages (out of %d)", n_lineages),
      y = "Number of GPs"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  # Apply Log Scaling if requested
  if (log_y) {
    # We use log1p or add a small constant if there are 0 counts to avoid -Inf
    p <- p + scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_log(), 
                           breaks = scales::breaks_log()) +
         ylab("Number of GPs (Log10 Scale)")
  }

  return(p)
}

# Execution with Log Scale
p_line_log <- plot_gp_percent_line_sensitivity(lineage_proportions_mat, 
                                               percent_vec = c(1e-3, 0.01, 0.1, 0.15, 0.5),
                                               loading_fixed = 0.1,
                                               log_y = FALSE)

print(p_line_log)

# Save the figure
ggsave("gp_threshold_line_comparison.pdf", plot = p_line_log, width = 8, height = 8)
```


Alluvial plot:

```{r}
colnames(F_pm_filtered) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(F_pm_filtered)))
# take out the most relevant genes for a selected GP
find_gene_GP <- function(GP, top_n = 2){
  if (!GP %in% colnames(F_pm_filtered)) {
    stop("GP not found in the data.")
  }
  
  gene_loadings <- F_pm_filtered[, GP]
  top_genes <- names(sort(abs(gene_loadings), decreasing = TRUE))[1:top_n]
  
  # if it is positive add +, if negative add -
  top_genes <- ifelse(gene_loadings[top_genes] >= 0, 
                      paste0(top_genes, "+"),
                      paste0(top_genes, "-"))
  return(top_genes)
}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggalluvial)
library(dplyr)

plot_gp_lineage_alluvial <- function(lineage_proportions_mat,
                                     percent_threshold = 0.1,
                                     loading_threshold = 0.1,
                                     gp_names = NULL,
                                     lineage_names = NULL,
                                     shared_levels_to_show = 2:6, 
                                     max_per_level = 100,
                                     base_size = 12,
                                     gp_text_size = 2,
                                     lineage_text_size = 3.5, # Size for CD4, CD8, etc.
                                     top_n_genes = 2,
                                     title = NULL) {
  
  mat <- as.matrix(lineage_proportions_mat)
  n_lineages <- nrow(mat)
  
  # 1. Custom Lineage Colors
  my_lin_cols <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1

  if (is.null(lineage_names)) lineage_names <- rownames(mat) %||% paste0("L", 1:n_lineages)
  if (is.null(gp_names)) gp_names <- colnames(mat) %||% paste0("GP", 1:ncol(mat))
  rownames(mat) <- lineage_names
  colnames(mat) <- gp_names

  # 2. Edge list and Thresholding
  df <- as.data.frame(as.table(mat))
  colnames(df) <- c("lineage", "gp", "prop")
  df <- df %>% filter(prop >= percent_threshold)
  
  # 3. Calculate Sharedness and Filter
  set.seed(42) 
  gp_stats <- df %>%
    group_by(gp) %>%
    summarize(
      n_shared = n(),
      profile = paste(as.integer(lineage_names %in% lineage), collapse = "")
    ) %>%
    filter(n_shared %in% shared_levels_to_show) %>% 
    group_by(n_shared) %>%
    slice_sample(n = max_per_level) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(desc(n_shared), desc(profile))

  if (nrow(gp_stats) == 0) stop("No GPs found for the selected shared_levels_to_show.")

  # 4. Append Leading Genes to GP Labels
  gp_labels <- sapply(as.character(gp_stats$gp), function(x) {
    genes <- find_gene_GP(x, top_n = top_n_genes)
    paste0(x, ": ", paste(genes, collapse = ", "))
  })
  gp_stats$gp_annotated <- gp_labels[as.character(gp_stats$gp)]

  # 5. Data Prep
  df <- df %>%
    filter(gp %in% gp_stats$gp) %>%
    left_join(gp_stats, by = "gp") %>%
    mutate(
      gp = factor(gp_annotated, levels = gp_stats$gp_annotated),
      lineage = factor(lineage, levels = lineage_names)
    )

  # 6. Color Mapping
  shared_cols_ramp <- colorRampPalette(c("#DEEBF7", "#08306B"))(n_lineages)
  gp_to_color <- setNames(shared_cols_ramp[gp_stats$n_shared], gp_stats$gp_annotated)
  shared_num_to_color <- setNames(shared_cols_ramp, as.character(1:n_lineages))
  all_colors <- c(my_lin_cols, shared_num_to_color, gp_to_color)

  if (is.null(title)) title <- "GP Sharedness & Leading Genes"

  # 7. Plotting
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(axis1 = lineage, axis2 = gp, y = 1)) +
    # Flows
    geom_alluvium(aes(fill = lineage), width = 1/10, alpha = 0.4, knot.pos = 0.4) +
    
    # Stratum Boxes
    geom_stratum(aes(fill = after_stat(stratum)), width = 1/6, color = "white") +
    
    # Unified Text Layer: Conditional Size
    # after_stat(stratum) checks the label; if it's a lineage, use the bigger size
    geom_text(stat = "stratum", aes(
                label = after_stat(stratum),
                size = after_stat(ifelse(stratum %in% lineage_names, 
                                         lineage_text_size, 
                                         gp_text_size))
              )) +
    
    # Need to tell ggplot not to create a legend for the text sizes
    scale_size_identity() + 
    
    scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Lineage", "GP"), expand = c(.1, .1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = all_colors,
      breaks = as.character(shared_levels_to_show),
      name = "Number of Lineages\nShared (1-6)",
      guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 5))
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = paste0("Excluding GPs shared by all ", n_lineages, " lineages"),
      x = NULL, y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  return(p)
}

`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b

p_annotated <- plot_gp_lineage_alluvial(
  lineage_proportions_mat, 
  percent_threshold = 0.1,
  top_n_genes = 2,
  gp_text_size = 4,
  lineage_text_size = 5,
  shared_levels_to_show = 2:6, 
  base_size = 14
)

ggsave("gp_alluvial_with_genes.pdf", plot = p_annotated, height = 16, width = 15)
```



## Network plot for the selected GPs and their top associated genes:

```{r}
GPs_of_interest <- paste0("K", c(25, 26, 10, 43, 12, 58, 171, 9, 79, 35, 72, 177, 161, 152, 162, 36, 56))

top_genes <- apply(F_pm_filtered_norm_subset, 2, function(x) {
  top_genes <- names(sort(abs(x), decreasing = TRUE))[1:5]
  return(top_genes)
})
top_5_pos <- apply(F_pm_filtered_norm_subset,2,function(x) {
  idx <- order(abs(x),decreasing=TRUE)[1:5]
  idx <- idx[x[idx] > 0]
  top_genes_pos <- names(x)[idx]
  return(top_genes_pos)
})
top_5_neg <- apply(F_pm_filtered_norm_subset,2,function(x) {
  idx <- order(abs(x),decreasing=TRUE)[1:5]
  idx <- idx[x[idx] < 0]
  top_genes_neg <- names(x)[idx]
  return(top_genes_neg)
})
names(top_5_pos) <- GPs_of_interest
names(top_5_neg) <- GPs_of_interest
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidygraph)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)

# 1. Prepare edges
all_edges_sorted <- all_edges %>%
  arrange(Type, GP, Gene) 

# 2. Build graph and add node metadata
graph <- as_tbl_graph(all_edges_sorted) %>%
  activate(nodes) %>%
  mutate(
    NodeGroup = ifelse(name %in% all_edges$GP, "GP", "Gene"),
    Importance = centrality_degree()
  ) %>%
  # Join quadrant info from label_df
  left_join(
    label_df %>% select(SYMBOL, lab_col, quad) %>% rename(name = SYMBOL),
    by = "name"
  ) %>%
  # Now 'quad' and 'lab_col' exist within the nodes data frame
  mutate(
    QuadGroup = ifelse(NodeGroup == "GP", as.character(quad), "Gene"),
    gp_label = ifelse(NodeGroup == "GP", name, "")
  )

# 3. Setup color scale for the legend
quad_colors <- label_df %>% 
  select(quad, lab_col) %>% 
  distinct() %>% 
  { setNames(.$lab_col, as.character(.$quad)) }

all_colors <- c(quad_colors, "Gene" = "#666666")

# 4. Plotting
p_network <- ggraph(graph, layout = "nicely") +  
  geom_edge_link(aes(color = Color), alpha = 0.4, width = 0.6) +
  
  # Gene nodes
  geom_node_point(
    aes(filter = (NodeGroup == "Gene"), color = QuadGroup),
    shape = 16, size = 2, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # GP nodes (Large squares)
  geom_node_point(
    aes(filter = (NodeGroup == "GP"), color = QuadGroup),
    shape = 15, size = 12, alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  
  # Labels
  geom_node_text(
    aes(filter = (NodeGroup == "GP"), label = gp_label),
    color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3
  ) +
  geom_node_text(
    aes(filter = (NodeGroup == "Gene"), label = name),
    repel = TRUE, size = 2.5, color = "black", max.overlaps = 20
  ) +
  
  # Scales
  scale_edge_color_identity() + 
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Quadrant Group",
    values = all_colors,
    breaks = names(quad_colors) # Hides 'Gene' from legend
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "GP-Gene Signature Network",
    subtitle = "Nodes colored by Quadrant classification",
    caption = "Red: Positive | Blue: Negative"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5, shape = 15)))

# Save
ggsave("GP_Gene_Network.pdf", plot = p_network, width = 8, height = 8)
```


## GSEA results for the selected GPs:

```{r}
library(readxl)
gsea_NES_results <- read_excel("data/gsea_results.xlsx", 
                           sheet = "NES")
gsea_qval_results <- read_excel("data/gsea_results.xlsx", 
                           sheet = "qval")
gene_set_info <- read_excel("data/gsea_results.xlsx",
                           sheet = "gene_set_info") 
```

```{r}
# selected columns to be the considered GPs
gsea_NES_results_selected <- gsea_NES_results[, c("gene_set", GPs_of_interest)]
gsea_qval_results_selected <- gsea_qval_results[, c("gene_set", GPs_of_interest)]

# remove rows with qval > 0.01 in all GPs
gsea_qval_results_selected <- gsea_qval_results_selected %>%
  filter(if_any(all_of(GPs_of_interest), ~ .x <= 0.01))

gene_set_names_selected <- gene_set_info$name[match(gsea_qval_results_selected$gene_set, gene_set_info$id)]
```

```{r}
library(stringr)

keep_terms <- c(
  "CD8", "CD4", "TCELL", "T_CELL", "TCONV", "TREG",
  "T_HELPER", "TH1", "TH2", "TH17", "TFH", "T_FH",
  "NAIVE_T", "MEMORY_T", "EFFECTOR_T", "CYTOTOXIC_T", "EXHAUSTED_T",
  "GDT", "GAMMADELTA"
)

pattern <- regex(
  paste0("(^|_)(", paste(keep_terms, collapse="|"), ")(_|$)"),
  ignore_case = TRUE
)

gene_set_names_keep <- gene_set_names_selected[str_detect(gene_set_names_selected, pattern)]
gene_set_names_keep
```

```{r}
gsea_NES_results_selected_filtered <- gsea_NES_results_selected %>%
  filter(gene_set %in% gsea_qval_results_selected$gene_set) %>%
  filter(gene_set %in% gene_set_info$id[match(gene_set_names_keep, gene_set_info$name)])

gsea_qval_results_selected_filtered <- gsea_qval_results_selected %>%
  filter(gene_set %in% gsea_NES_results_selected_filtered$gene_set) 
```

```{r}
top_n <- 5

long <- gsea_qval_results_selected_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(-gene_set, names_to = "gp", values_to = "qval") %>%
  left_join(
    gsea_NES_results_selected_filtered %>%
      pivot_longer(-gene_set, names_to = "gp", values_to = "NES"),
    by = c("gene_set", "gp")
  )

gp_order <- setdiff(colnames(gsea_qval_results_selected_filtered), "gene_set")

picked <- character(0)
out_list <- vector("list", length(gp_order))

for (i in seq_along(gp_order)) {
  gp <- gp_order[i]

  cand <- long %>%
    filter(gp == !!gp, !gene_set %in% picked, !is.na(qval)) %>%
    arrange(qval)

  take <- cand %>% slice_head(n = top_n)

  out_list[[i]] <- take %>% mutate(rank_within_gp = row_number())
  picked <- c(picked, take$gene_set)
}

selected_long_unique <- bind_rows(out_list)

stopifnot(n_distinct(selected_long_unique$gene_set) == nrow(selected_long_unique))

selected_long_unique
```

```{r}
map_df <- gene_set_info %>%
  distinct(id, name)

plot_df <- selected_long_unique %>%
  left_join(map_df, by = c("gene_set" = "id")) %>%
  mutate(name = ifelse(is.na(name), gene_set, name))  

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  mutate(name_unique = if (n() == 1) name else paste0(name, " (", gene_set, ")")) %>%
  ungroup()

nes_mat <- plot_df %>%
  select(name_unique, gp, NES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = gp, values_from = NES) %>%
  column_to_rownames("name_unique") %>%
  as.matrix()

max_abs <- max(abs(nes_mat), na.rm = TRUE)
bk <- seq(-max_abs, max_abs, length.out = 101)
cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#4575B4", "white", "#D73027"))(100)

# make NA zero
nes_mat[is.na(nes_mat)] <- 0

pheatmap(
  nes_mat,
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  border_color = "grey70",   # grid lines
  fontsize_row = 6,          # y-axis text smaller
  fontsize_col = 9,          # optional
  main = "Selected gene sets in each GP: NES",
  filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_GPs_GSEA_heatmap.pdf",
  width = 8,
  height = 8
)
```

## More GSEA results for the selected GPs:

```{r}
# read GSEA_signatures_select_toplot.csv
df_sig = read.csv("data/GSEA_signatures_select_toplot.csv", header = T, sep = ",")

# rename factor from Fx to Kx for consistency
df_sig <- df_sig %>%
  mutate(factor = str_replace(factor, "^F", "K"))

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

df_plot <- df_sig %>%
  mutate(
    pathway = factor(pathway, levels = unique(pathway)),
    factor  = factor(factor,  levels = unique(factor)),
    log10padj = -log10(padj)
  )


# 1. Prepare the color vector for the Y-axis (Factors)
# Get the unique factors in the order they appear in df_plot
y_factors <- levels(df_plot$factor)

# Match colors from your previous label_df
# Note: Ensure label_df$SYMBOL matches your factor names (e.g., "K26")
y_colors <- label_df$lab_col[match(y_factors, label_df$SYMBOL)]
y_colors[is.na(y_colors)] <- "black" # Default to black if not found

# 2. Update the Plot
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = pathway, y = factor)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = NES, color = log10padj)) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(name = "-log10(p-adj)") +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 10)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    # Apply the quadrant colors to Y-axis text
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = y_colors, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(size = "NES", x = "Pathway", y = "Gene Program")

ggsave("DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_GPs_GSEA_dotplot.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)

```



## Heatmap of the selected GPs across level-2 categories:

Next, produce a heatmap of the selected GPs for some selected level-2 categories.

```{r}
L_pm_filtered_subset <- L_pm_filtered[c(CD4_cells, CD8_cells), GPs_of_interest, drop = FALSE]
level_2_categories <- unique(seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset), seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)])
global_median <- apply(L_pm_filtered_subset, 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)

reference_median_loading <- matrix(
  rep(global_median, each = length(level_2_categories)),
  nrow = length(level_2_categories),
  byrow = FALSE,
  dimnames = list(level_2_categories, GPs_of_interest)
)
```


```{r}
# compute median loading for each level-2 category
median_loading_matrix <- sapply(GPs_of_interest, function(gp) {
  sapply(level_2_categories, function(cat) {
    cells_in_cat <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2 == cat]
    median(L_pm_filtered_subset[cells_in_cat, gp], na.rm = TRUE)
  })
})
rownames(median_loading_matrix) <- level_2_categories
```

```{r}
log2fc_matrix <- log2((median_loading_matrix) / (reference_median_loading))

log2fc_cap <- pmax(pmin(log2fc_matrix, 1), -1)
factor_levels <- colnames(log2fc_cap)
factor_levels <- factor_levels[order(as.numeric(sub("^K", "", factor_levels)))]
level2_names <- rownames(log2fc_cap)
level1_prefix <- sub("_.*$", "", level2_names)
level2_levels <- level2_names[order(level1_prefix, level2_names)]
df_long <- as.data.frame(log2fc_cap) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Level2") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -Level2,
    names_to  = "Factor",
    values_to = "log2fc"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Factor = factor(Factor, levels = factor_levels),
    Level2 = factor(Level2, levels = level2_levels)
  )

p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Factor, y = Level2, fill = log2fc)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey30", size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",
    midpoint = 0, oob = scales::squish, name = "log2 FC"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(
    title = "log2 fold-change (level-2 category median loading vs global median loading)",
    x = "", y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 7) +
  theme(
    panel.grid  = element_blank(),
    # axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.ticks   = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(p, filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_GPs_heatmap_level2.pdf", width = 6, height = 10)
```

Produce a structure plot for level-2 categories:

```{r}
library(fastTopics)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(colorspace)

topics_global <- colnames(L_pm_filtered_subset)[order(colMeans(L_pm_filtered_subset))]
k <- ncol(L_pm_filtered_subset)
base_cols <- if (k <= 20) {
  fastTopics:::kelly()[-1][1:k]        
} else {
  glasbey()[-1][1:k]
}

cols_global <- lighten(desaturate(base_cols, amount = 0.40), amount = 0.12)
names(cols_global) <- colnames(L_pm_filtered_subset)

# 0) cell_type: annotation_level2 aligned to L rows
cell_type <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[
  match(rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset), seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]

# helper: balanced sampling per level2
sample_cells_by_cluster <- function(cell_ids, cell_type_vec,
```
