```{r}
                                    max_per_cluster = 3000,
                                    small_keep_all = 300,
                                    seed = 123) {
  set.seed(seed)
  unique_ct <- unique(cell_type_vec)

  unlist(lapply(unique_ct, function(ct) {
    cells_in_ct <- cell_ids[cell_type_vec == ct]
    n <- length(cells_in_ct)

    if (n <= small_keep_all) {
      cells_in_ct
    } else if (n > max_per_cluster) {
      sample(cells_in_ct, max_per_cluster)
    } else {
      cells_in_ct
    }
  }), use.names = FALSE)
}

# 1) split to CD4 / CD8 by level2 name (adjust pattern if needed)
cell_ids <- rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset)

is_cd4 <- str_detect(cell_type, regex("CD4", ignore_case = TRUE))
is_cd8 <- str_detect(cell_type, regex("CD8", ignore_case = TRUE))

cd4_cells_all <- cell_ids[is_cd4 & !is.na(is_cd4)]
cd8_cells_all <- cell_ids[is_cd8 & !is.na(is_cd8)]

# 2) sample within each group, keeping tiny clusters intact
cd4_type <- cell_type[match(cd4_cells_all, cell_ids)]
cd8_type <- cell_type[match(cd8_cells_all, cell_ids)]

cd4_cells <- sample_cells_by_cluster(
  cd4_cells_all, cd4_type,
  max_per_cluster = 10000, small_keep_all = 300, seed = 123
)
cd8_cells <- sample_cells_by_cluster(
  cd8_cells_all, cd8_type,
  max_per_cluster = 10000, small_keep_all = 300, seed = 123
)

cd4_type_s <- cd4_type[match(cd4_cells, cd4_cells_all)]
# droplevels
cd4_type_s <- factor(cd4_type_s, levels = unique(cd4_type_s))
cd8_type_s <- cd8_type[match(cd8_cells, cd8_cells_all)]
# droplevels
cd8_type_s <- factor(cd8_type_s, levels = unique(cd8_type_s))

# 3) two structure plots
p_cd4 <- structure_plot(L_pm_filtered_subset[cd4_cells, , drop = FALSE],
                        gap = 30, n = 5000,
                        topics = topics_global,
                        colors = cols_global,
                        grouping = cd4_type_s) +
  labs(title = "CD4", y = "membership", color = "", fill = "") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(nrow = 2))

p_cd8 <- structure_plot(L_pm_filtered_subset[cd8_cells, , drop = FALSE],
                        gap = 30, n = 5000,
                        topics = topics_global,
                        colors = cols_global,
                        grouping = cd8_type_s) +
  labs(title = "CD8", y = "membership", color = "", fill = "") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(nrow = 2))

p_cd4
p_cd8
```


```{r}
# 0) cell_type: annotation_level2 aligned to L rows
cell_type <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[
  match(rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset), seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]
cell_group <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[
  match(rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset), seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]

# 1) split to CD4 / CD8 by level2 name (adjust pattern if needed)
cell_ids <- rownames(L_pm_filtered_subset)

is_cd4 <- str_detect(cell_type, regex("CD4", ignore_case = TRUE))
is_cd8 <- str_detect(cell_type, regex("CD8", ignore_case = TRUE))

cd4_cells_all <- cell_ids[is_cd4 & !is.na(is_cd4)]
cd8_cells_all <- cell_ids[is_cd8 & !is.na(is_cd8)]

# 2) sample within each group, keeping tiny clusters intact
cd4_group <- cell_group[match(cd4_cells_all, cell_ids)]
cd8_group <- cell_group[match(cd8_cells_all, cell_ids)]

cd4_group_s <- cd4_group[match(cd4_cells, cd4_cells_all)]
# droplevels
cd4_group_s <- factor(cd4_group_s, levels = unique(cd4_group_s))
cd8_group_s <- cd8_group[match(cd8_cells, cd8_cells_all)]
# droplevels
cd8_group_s <- factor(cd8_group_s, levels = unique(cd8_group_s))

# 3) two structure plots
p_cd4_group <- structure_plot(L_pm_filtered_subset[cd4_cells, , drop = FALSE],
                        gap = 30, n = 5000,
                        topics = topics_global,
                        colors = cols_global,
                        grouping = cd4_group_s) +
  labs(title = "CD4", y = "membership", color = "", fill = "") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,3)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(nrow = 2))

p_cd8_group <- structure_plot(L_pm_filtered_subset[cd8_cells, , drop = FALSE],
                        gap = 30, n = 5000,
                        topics = topics_global,
                        colors = cols_global,
                        grouping = cd8_group_s) +
  labs(title = "CD8", y = "membership", color = "", fill = "") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,3)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(nrow = 2))

p_cd4_group
ggsave(p_cd4_group, filename = "structure_plot_CD4_group.pdf", width = 8, height = 5)
p_cd8_group
ggsave(p_cd8_group, filename = "structure_plot_CD8_group.pdf", width = 8, height = 5)
```


## Cell-level heatmap for the selected GPs:

Produce a cell-level heatmap of the selected GPs for CD4 and CD8 cells:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(stringr)

# ==========================================
# 1. Initial Data Preparation & Alignment
# ==========================================
L <- L_pm_filtered_subset  # cells x GP matrix
cell_ids <- rownames(L)

# Match full metadata for the cells
cell_type <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[
  match(cell_ids, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]

# ==========================================
# 2. Sampling Logic (with total budget)
# ==========================================
set.seed(123)
total_budget     <- 8000
small_keep_all   <- 150   # clusters <= this keep all cells
min_per_cluster  <- 150   # clusters larger than small_keep_all get at least this many
max_per_cluster  <- 600   # optional cap per cluster

cells_by_ct <- split(cell_ids, cell_type)
sizes <- sapply(cells_by_ct, length)

# Initial allocation
alloc <- ifelse(sizes <= small_keep_all, sizes, pmin(min_per_cluster, sizes))
remaining <- total_budget - sum(alloc)

# Distribute remaining budget proportionally
if (remaining > 0) {
  room <- pmax(pmin(sizes, max_per_cluster) - alloc, 0)
  if (sum(room) > 0) {
    extra <- floor(remaining * room / sum(room))
    alloc <- alloc + extra
    
    # Fill greedily if there is leftover budget due to flooring
    leftover <- total_budget - sum(alloc)
    if (leftover > 0) {
      idx <- order(room, decreasing = TRUE)
      for (j in idx) {
        if (leftover <= 0) break
        addable <- pmin(room[j] - extra[j], leftover)
        if (addable > 0) {
          alloc[j] <- alloc[j] + addable
          leftover <- leftover - addable
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# Execute sampling based on final allocation
sampled_cells <- unlist(mapply(function(v, m) {
  if (length(v) <= m) v else sample(v, m)
}, cells_by_ct, pmin(alloc, sizes), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), use.names = FALSE)

# ==========================================
# 3. Strict Data Cleaning 
# ==========================================
cell_group_s  <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group[match(sampled_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)]
cell_level2_s <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[match(sampled_cells, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)]

# Standardize strings
cell_group_s  <- trimws(tolower(as.character(cell_group_s)))
cell_level2_s <- trimws(as.character(cell_level2_s))

# NEW: Extract Level 1 from Level 2
cell_level1_s <- sapply(strsplit(cell_level2_s, "[_.]"), `[`, 1)

# --- FILTER LOGIC ---
# Identify cells where Level2 ends with ".w" followed by anything (e.g., .w1, .w2)
# or simply contains ".w" as a suffix.
is_w_cell <- grepl("\\.w", cell_level2_s) 

# Find valid indices (added !is_w_cell condition)
valid_idx <- which(
  !is.na(cell_level2_s) & 
  !tolower(cell_level2_s) %in% c("", "na", "nan") & 
  cell_group_s %in% c("resting", "activated") &
  !is_w_cell
)

final_cells  <- sampled_cells[valid_idx]
final_group  <- cell_group_s[valid_idx]
final_level1 <- cell_level1_s[valid_idx]
final_level2 <- cell_level2_s[valid_idx]

# ==========================================
# 4. Reordering (Updated for Nested Sorting)
# ==========================================
# Sort by Group -> Level 1 -> Level 2 to keep the visual blocks clean
col_order <- order(final_group, final_level1, final_level2)

final_cells  <- final_cells[col_order]
final_group  <- final_group[col_order]
final_level1 <- final_level1[col_order]
final_level2 <- final_level2[col_order]

# ==========================================
# 5. Calculate Log2FC Matrix
# ==========================================
# Subset the main matrix using the fully cleaned and sorted cell IDs
L_sub <- L[final_cells, , drop = FALSE]
M_raw <- t(L_sub)
# Calculate median baseline and log2 fold-change
mu <- apply(M_raw, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
pc <- 1e-10
M_fc <- log2((M_raw + pc) / (mu + pc))
# Cap extreme values to improve visual contrast
cap <- 2
M_fc_cap <- pmax(pmin(M_fc, cap), -cap)

# ==========================================
# 6. Setup Legends & Annotations (Refined)
# ==========================================
# 1. Ensure columns are factors and ordered correctly for the plot stack
# (Bottom to Top in data frame = Heatmap side to Outward side)
ann_col <- data.frame(
  Level2 = factor(final_level2),
  Level1 = factor(final_level1),
  Group  = factor(final_group, levels = c("resting", "activated"))
)
rownames(ann_col) <- colnames(M_fc_cap)

# 2. Extract specific levels present in this sampled data
present_level1 <- levels(ann_col$Level1)
present_level2 <- levels(ann_col$Level2)

# 3. Map Level 1 Colors (Standard lookup)
level1_cols_used <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1
level1_cols_used <- level1_cols_used[names(level1_cols_used) %in% present_level1]

# 4. Map Level 2 Colors (Robust lookup)
level2_cols_used <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level2

# CRITICAL FIX: Ensure we only take names that exist in our current final_level2
# We filter the library to only include what is present in our current plot
level2_cols_final <- level2_cols_used[names(level2_cols_used) %in% present_level2]

# 5. Safety Check: If levels in data are not in the library, assign grey80
missing_l2 <- setdiff(present_level2, names(level2_cols_final))
if(length(missing_l2) > 0) {
  # Add missing levels as grey
  extra_grey <- setNames(rep("grey80", length(missing_l2)), missing_l2)
  level2_cols_final <- c(level2_cols_final, extra_grey)
}

# 6. Consolidate all annotation colors
ann_colors <- list(
  Group  = c("resting" = "#08519C", "activated" = "#E53800"),
  Level1 = level1_cols_used,
  Level2 = level2_cols_final
)

# ==========================================
# 7. Calculate Gaps and Plot
# ==========================================
# Add gaps between resting and activated
rle_g <- rle(as.character(ann_col$Group))
gaps_col <- cumsum(rle_g$lengths)
gaps_col <- gaps_col[-length(gaps_col)]

pheatmap(
  M_fc_cap,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  gaps_col = gaps_col,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#7A0177", "black", "#FFD700"))(101),
  breaks = seq(-cap, cap, length.out = 102),
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  fontsize_row = 7,         # Adjusted for readability
  fontface_row = "bold",    # Bold makes colors pop
  border_color = NA,
  annotation_col = ann_col, 
  annotation_colors = ann_colors,
  annotation_names_col = TRUE,
  useRaster = TRUE,
  main = "GP Log2FC: Nested Annotation (Group > Level1 > Level2)",
  filename = "DGE_CD4_vs_CD8_GPs_heatmap_cell_level_nested.pdf",
  width = 12, # Widened slightly to accommodate more legends
  height = 10
)
```




## Boxplot of the number of highly active GPs in activated/resting CD4 and CD8 cells:

Boxplot showing the number of highly active GPs in activated/resting CD4 and CD8 cells:

```{r}
gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition <- data.frame(
  Level1 = seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1,
  Condition = seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group,
  Active_Cell_Counts = rowSums(L_pm_filtered > 1e-1)
)

# restrict to CD4 and CD8
gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition <- gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition %>%
  filter(Level1 %in% c("CD4", "CD8"))

# restrict to activated and resting
gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition <- gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition %>%
  filter(Condition %in% c("activated", "resting"))

# Put side by side boxplots
p_boxplot <- ggplot(gp_active_cell_counts_Level1_Condition, aes(x = Condition, y = Active_Cell_Counts, fill = Condition)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Level1) +
  labs(title = "Number of Active GPs by Condition and Cell Type", x = "Condition", y = "Number of Active GPs") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

p_boxplot
```





## UMAP plots

```{r}
# Plot all UMAPs
umap_result <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "umap_result.rds"))
colnames(umap_result) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
umap_result <- umap_result[rownames(L_pm_filtered),]
df_umap <- as.data.frame(umap_result)
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scattermore)
})

set.seed(1)

# 1) move rownames -> cellID
df_umap2 <- df_umap %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("cellID")

# 2) join with metadata by cellID
plot_df <- df_umap2 %>%
  inner_join(
    seurat_meta_filtered %>% select(cellID, annotation_level1),
    by = "cellID"
  )

# 3) stratified subsample (keeps rare groups visible)
max_total <- 1000000
min_per_group <- 300
cap_per_group <- 20000

group_sizes <- plot_df %>% count(annotation_level1, name = "n")
G <- nrow(group_sizes)
base_per_group <- ceiling(max_total / max(G, 1))

sample_plan <- group_sizes %>%
  mutate(n_take = pmin(n, pmax(min_per_group, pmin(cap_per_group, base_per_group))))

plot_df_sub <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(annotation_level1) %>%
  group_modify(~ dplyr::slice_sample(.x, n = sample_plan$n_take[sample_plan$annotation_level1 == .y$annotation_level1])) %>%
  ungroup()

# 4) fast plot
p_umap_level1_group <- ggplot(plot_df_sub, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2)) +
  # Using scattermore for high-performance plotting
  scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(color = annotation_level1), pointsize = 1.2) +
  # Apply the specific color palette from your library
  scale_color_manual(values = ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP: Annotation Level 1",
    x = "UMAP 1", 
    y = "UMAP 2", 
    color = "Cell Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    # Make legend points larger so colors are easier to see
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "lines")
  ) +
  # Override aesthetic for legend to make points visible/larger in the key
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4)))

print(p_umap_level1_group)

ggsave(p_umap_level1_group, filename = "UMAP_level1_group.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
```

Let's only focus on gdT and CD8aa:

```{r}
# Filter to gdT and CD8aa
plot_df_sub_focused <- plot_df_sub %>%
  filter(annotation_level1 %in% c("gdT", "CD8aa"))

plot_df_sub_focused$annotation_level2 <- seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2[
  match(plot_df_sub_focused$cellID, seurat_meta_filtered$cellID)
]

p_umap_focused <- ggplot(plot_df_sub_focused, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2)) +
  scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(color = annotation_level2), pointsize = 1.2) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP: Focused on gdT and CD8aa",
    x = "UMAP 1", 
    y = "UMAP 2", 
    color = "Level 2 Clusters"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "lines")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4)))

print(p_umap_focused)
ggsave(p_umap_focused, filename = "UMAP_focused_gdT_CD8aa_level2.pdf", width = 7, height = 7)
```

```{r}
plot_loadings_on_umap_v2 <- function(umap, loading, factor_num,
                                     size = 0.25,
                                     show_bg = TRUE,
                                     bg_alpha = 0.05,
                                     bg_color = "grey90",
                                     clip_q = 0.99,
                                     top_q = NULL,
                                     viridis_option = "viridis",
                                     viridis_direction = 1) {
  stopifnot(is.matrix(umap) || is.data.frame(umap))
  df <- as.data.frame(umap)
  stopifnot(all(c("UMAP_1","UMAP_2") %in% colnames(df)))
  stopifnot(nrow(df) == length(loading))

  q_hi <- stats::quantile(loading, probs = clip_q, na.rm = TRUE)
  loading_clip <- pmin(loading, q_hi)

  if (!is.null(top_q)) {
    stopifnot(top_q > 0 && top_q < 1)
    thr <- stats::quantile(loading_clip, probs = top_q, na.rm = TRUE)
    loading_plot <- ifelse(loading_clip >= thr, loading_clip, NA_real_)
  } else {
    loading_plot <- loading_clip
  }

  df$loading_plot <- loading_plot

  p <- ggplot() +
    # --- background layer (raster) ---
    {
      if (show_bg)
        scattermore::geom_scattermore(
          data = df,
          mapping = aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2),
          pointsize = size,          # size in pixels
          color = bg_color,
          alpha = bg_alpha
        )
    } +
    # --- colored loading layer (raster) ---
    scattermore::geom_scattermore(
      data = df,
      mapping = aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, color = loading_plot),
      pointsize = size*2,
      alpha = 1,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(
      option   = viridis_option,
      direction = viridis_direction,
      limits   = c(0, q_hi),
      oob      = scales::squish,
      breaks   = scales::pretty_breaks(4),
      name     = paste0("K", factor_num, " loading")
    ) +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Factor ", factor_num),
      x = "", y = ""
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

  p
}
```

```{r}
current_loadings <- L_pm_filtered[plot_df_sub_focused$cellID, 3]
p_factor_loading <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(
    umap       = plot_df_sub_focused,   # Use the dataframe created earlier
    loading    = current_loadings,      # Vector of loading values for these cells
    factor_num = 3,
    size       = 0.4,
    show_bg    = TRUE,
    bg_alpha   = 0.06,
    clip_q     = 0.99,
    top_q      = NULL,
    viridis_option = "viridis",
    viridis_direction = 1
  )

ggsave(p_factor_loading, filename = "UMAP_factor3_loading_gdT_CD8aa.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)

current_loadings <- L_pm_filtered[plot_df_sub_focused$cellID, 29]
p_factor_loading <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(
    umap       = plot_df_sub_focused,   # Use the dataframe created earlier
    loading    = current_loadings,      # Vector of loading values for these cells
    factor_num = 29,
    size       = 0.4,
    show_bg    = TRUE,
    bg_alpha   = 0.06,
    clip_q     = 0.99,
    top_q      = NULL,
    viridis_option = "viridis",
    viridis_direction = 1
  )

ggsave(p_factor_loading, filename = "UMAP_factor29_loading_gdT_CD8aa.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)

current_loadings <- L_pm_filtered[plot_df_sub_focused$cellID, 6]
p_factor_loading <- plot_loadings_on_umap_v2(
    umap       = plot_df_sub_focused,   # Use the dataframe created earlier
    loading    = current_loadings,      # Vector of loading values for these cells
    factor_num = 6,
    size       = 0.4,
    show_bg    = TRUE,
    bg_alpha   = 0.06,
    clip_q     = 0.99,
    top_q      = NULL,
    viridis_option = "viridis",
    viridis_direction = 1
  )

ggsave(p_factor_loading, filename = "UMAP_factor6_loading_gdT_CD8aa.pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
```






# Relevance of TFs to GPs

```{r}
CD4_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD4_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
CD8_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD8_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
CD8aa_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD8aa_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
gdT_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/gdT_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
DN_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/DN_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
DP_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/DP_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
Treg_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/Treg_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
non_conv_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/nonconv_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
```

```{r}
threshold <- 0 # include all TFs
TF_highlights <- c(CD4_TF_list$Gene[CD4_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   CD8_TF_list$Gene[CD8_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   CD8aa_TF_list$Gene[CD8aa_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   gdT_TF_list$Gene[gdT_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   DN_TF_list$Gene[DN_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   DP_TF_list$Gene[DP_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   non_conv_TF_list$Gene[non_conv_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   Treg_TF_list$Gene[Treg_TF_list$auc >= threshold])

TF_highlights <- unique(TF_highlights)
```

```{r}
# find abs max in each column of F_pm_filtered
Df <- apply(F_pm_filtered, 2, function(x) max(abs(x), na.rm = TRUE))
F_pm_norm_col <- scale_cols(F_pm_filtered, 1/Df)
colnames(F_pm_norm_col) <- paste0("K", 1:ncol(F_pm_norm_col))
```

This time just look at the TF factors:

```{r}
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_norm_col[rownames(F_pm_norm_col) %in% TF_highlights, ]

# only include TFs with at least one factor > 0.4
keep <- apply((F_pm_TF), 1, function(v) any(v > 0.4, na.rm = TRUE))
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_TF[keep, , drop = FALSE]

# only include GPs with at least one TF factor > 0.4
keep_gp <- apply((F_pm_TF), 2, function(v) any(v > 0.4, na.rm = TRUE))
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_TF[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

pdf("TF_GP_heatmap.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
pheatmap(
  (F_pm_TF),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 7, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
  # add grey grid lines
  border_color = "grey10",
  fontsize_row = 6,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  main = "TF × GP loading heatmap (normalized; TFs only)",
  legend = TRUE
)
dev.off()
```


```{r}
compute_tf_rank_matrix <- function(
  F,
  TFs,
  mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw"),
  ties.method = "average",
  verbose = TRUE
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  if (is.null(rownames(F))) stop("F must have rownames (gene names).")
  if (is.null(colnames(F))) colnames(F) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(F)))

  TFs_in <- intersect(TFs, rownames(F))
  TFs_drop <- setdiff(TFs, TFs_in)

  if (verbose && length(TFs_drop) > 0) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Dropping %d TFs not found in rownames(F). Example: %s",
      length(TFs_drop),
      paste(head(TFs_drop, 5), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  if (length(TFs_in) == 0) stop("None of the requested TFs are present in rownames(F).")

  score_mat <- switch(
    mode,
    positive = pmax(F, 0),
    abs      = abs(F),
    raw      = F
  )

  # rank within each column (GP): higher score => smaller rank (1 = best)
  rank_all <- apply(score_mat, 2, function(x) rank(-x, ties.method = ties.method, na.last = "keep"))

  # subset rows to TFs
  rank_tf <- rank_all[TFs_in, , drop = FALSE]

  list(
    rank = rank_tf,       # matrix: TF x GP
    TFs_used = TFs_in,
    TFs_dropped = TFs_drop
  )
}

rk_obj <- compute_tf_rank_matrix(F_pm_norm_col, TF_highlights, mode = "pos")
rank_tf <- rk_obj$rank

library(pheatmap)
rank_max <- 100
rank_clip <- pmin(rank_tf, rank_max)
keep <- apply(rank_clip, 1, function(v) any(v < rank_max, na.rm = TRUE))
rank_clip <- rank_clip[keep, , drop = FALSE]
pal <- colorRampPalette(c("red", "white"))(10)
bk  <- c(1, seq(10, 100, by = 10))

rank_filter <- 20

# keep only GPs that have at least one TF with rank < rank_filter
keep_gp <- apply(rank_clip, 2, function(v) any(v < rank_filter, na.rm = TRUE))
rank_clip <- rank_clip[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

# keep only TFs that have at least one GP with rank < rank_filter
keep_tf <- apply(rank_clip, 1, function(v) any(v < rank_filter, na.rm = TRUE))
rank_clip <- rank_clip[keep_tf, , drop = FALSE]

pheatmap(
  mat = rank_clip,
  color = pal,
  breaks = bk,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,          
  show_colnames = TRUE,          
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontface_row = "bold",
  fontsize_col = 7,
  main = sprintf("TF × GP rank heatmap (pos; ranks 1–%d)", rank_max),
  # --- Try these adjustments ---
  border_color = "grey80",     # Use a lighter grey for better visibility
  # -----------------------------
  legend_breaks = seq(1, rank_max, by = 10),
  legend_labels = as.character(seq(1, rank_max, by = 10)),
  filename = "TF_GP_rank_heatmap.pdf",
  width = 14,
  height = 12
)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

.compute_tf_rank_score <- function(F, tf, mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw")) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  if (is.null(rownames(F))) stop("F must have rownames (gene names).")
  if (!tf %in% rownames(F)) stop(sprintf("TF '%s' not found in rownames(F).", tf))

  G <- ncol(F)
  cols <- colnames(F)
  if (is.null(cols)) cols <- paste0("K", seq_len(G))

  score_mat <- switch(
    mode,
    positive = pmax(F, 0),
    abs      = abs(F),
    raw      = F
  )

  # Rank within each column: higher score -> smaller rank (rank=1 is best)
  rank_mat <- apply(score_mat, 2, function(x) rank(-x, ties.method = "average", na.last = "keep"))
  tf_rank <- as.numeric(rank_mat[tf, ])

  data.frame(
    GEP  = factor(cols, levels = cols),
    rank = tf_rank,
    tf   = tf,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

plot_tf_rank_bars <- function(
    F,
    TFs,
    mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw"),
    rank_max = 100,
    custom_x_breaks = NULL, 
    x_label_every = 10,
    facet = TRUE,
    alpha = 0.9,
    n_label_best = 1,
    # 3. New: Take a custom color vector (named or unnamed)
    tf_colors = NULL 
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  # .compute_tf_rank_score helper assumed to exist
  df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(TFs, function(tf) .compute_tf_rank_score(F, tf, mode = mode)))

  # Ensure numeric rank; drop missing ranks
  df$rank <- as.numeric(df$rank)
  df <- df[is.finite(df$rank), , drop = FALSE]

  # Update column name to GP
  colnames(df)[colnames(df) == "GEP"] <- "GP"
  levs <- levels(df$GP)
  df$x <- as.integer(df$GP)

  # "Skyscraper" logic
  df$rank_clip <- pmin(df$rank, rank_max)
  df$height <- rank_max - df$rank_clip

  # --- X-axis Handling ---
  if (!is.null(custom_x_breaks)) {
    x_breaks <- which(levs %in% custom_x_breaks)
    x_labels <- levs[x_breaks]
  } else {
    keep_idx <- seq(1, length(levs), by = x_label_every)
    x_breaks <- keep_idx
    x_labels <- levs[keep_idx]
  }

  # --- Y-axis Handling ---
  y_breaks <- seq(0, rank_max, by = 25)
  y_labels <- rank_max - y_breaks
  y_labels[y_labels == 0] <- 1

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = height, fill = tf)) +
    geom_col(alpha = alpha, width = 0.9) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, labels = x_labels) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = y_breaks,
      labels = y_labels,
      limits = c(0, rank_max * 1.2) 
    ) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("TF rank skyline across GPs (top %d)", rank_max),
      x = "GP",
      y = "Rank within each GP (1 = highest)"
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = if (facet) "none" else "right",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, color = "black"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines") 
    )

  # --- Apply Custom Colors if provided ---
  if (!is.null(tf_colors)) {
    p <- p + scale_fill_manual(values = tf_colors)
  }

  if (facet) {
    p <- p + facet_wrap(~tf, ncol = 1, scales = "fixed")
  }

  # --- Labeling logic with ggrepel ---
  if (n_label_best > 0 && nrow(df) > 0) {
    best_df <- df %>%
      filter(rank <= rank_max) %>% 
      group_by(tf) %>%
      slice_min(order_by = rank, n = n_label_best, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(label = as.character(GP))

    p <- p + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = best_df,
      aes(x = x, y = height, label = label),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      vjust = -0.5, 
      size = 3.5,
      fontface = "bold",
      box.padding = 0.3,
      point.padding = 0.2,
      direction = "y",
      segment.color = "grey50"
    )
  }

  return(p)
}

x_breaks <- paste0("K", seq(0, ncol(F_pm_norm_col), by = 10))
x_breaks[1] <- "K1" # Ensure the first GP is labeled as K1

p1 <- plot_tf_rank_bars(
  F = F_pm_norm_col,
  TFs = c("Tcf7", "Foxp3", "Sox13"),
  mode = "pos",
  rank_max = 100,
  custom_x_breaks = x_breaks,
  facet = TRUE,
  n_label_best = 5,
  tf_colors = c("#1b9e57", "#1b9e57", "#1b9e57")
)

p2 <- plot_tf_rank_bars(
  F = F_pm_norm_col,
  TFs = c("Runx3", "Gata3", "Foxn3"),
  mode = "pos",
  rank_max = 100,
  custom_x_breaks = x_breaks,
  facet = TRUE,
  n_label_best = 5,
  tf_colors = c("#1b9e57", "#1b9e57", "#1b9e57")
)

ggsave(p1, filename = "TF_rank_skyline_pos.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)
ggsave(p2, filename = "TF_rank_skyline_pos_2.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)
```

## CITE-seq analysis

Define a few set of markers by threshold logFC in each GP:

```{r}
# protein_flash_summary_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_flash_selected_summary_lognorm.rds"))
# D_lognorm <- diag(1 / apply(protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))))
# Protein_F_pm <- protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm %*% D_lognorm
# factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
# colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("K", factors_considered)

Protein_F_pm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_projection_OLS_lognorm.rds"))
cells_citeseq <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$cite_seq]
```

Take only good quality proteins and proteins with name starting with Isotype

```{r}
isotype_proteins <- grep("^Isotype", rownames(Protein_F_pm), value = TRUE)
proteins_quality <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "TableS4_citeseq_qc_20250513.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, skip = 1)
good_proteins <- proteins_quality$protein[proteins_quality$classification == "good"]
# put some proteins of interest into good_proteins
good_proteins <- c(good_proteins, "IL2RA.CD25", "ITB7", "CD69")
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm[!rownames(Protein_F_pm) %in% c(isotype_proteins), ]
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm[rownames(Protein_F_pm) %in% good_proteins, ]

exclude_proteins <- c("CD19", "CD34", "CD45.1","CD45.2", "CD138", "TCRVA2", "TER119")
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm[!rownames(Protein_F_pm) %in% exclude_proteins, ]

thy11_proteins <- grep("THY1.1", rownames(Protein_F_pm), value = TRUE)
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm[!rownames(Protein_F_pm) %in% thy11_proteins, ]

# redo the normalization
D_lognorm <- diag(1 / apply(Protein_F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))))
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm %*% D_lognorm
# colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("K", factors_considered)
colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("K", 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm))
```

## Protein heatmap


Produce a giant heatmap of everything

```{r}
library(pheatmap)
bk <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101)
cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#4575B4", "white", "#D73027"))(100)
# this is a giant heatmap, we should make the size large 
pdf("Protein_Programs_heatmap_all.pdf", width = 20, height = 40)
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  t(Protein_F_pm),
  main = "Protein programs (normalized)",
  # color scale, blue negative, white zero, red positive
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk,
  border_color = "black"
)
dev.off()
```



Heatmap of GP of interest from the other figures:

```{r}
library(pheatmap)
# make it sparse
threshold_protein <- 0.2
Protein_F_pm_sparse <- ifelse(
  abs(Protein_F_pm) >= threshold_protein,
  Protein_F_pm,
  0
)

# make sure all proteins are at least strongly positively expressed in one GP
all(apply(Protein_F_pm_sparse, 1, function(v) any(v >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE)))
# take out GPs of interest
keep_gp_figure2 <- c(1,3,22,29,30,58,68)
keep_gp_figure3 <- c(25, 26, 10, 43, 12, 58, 171, 9, 79, 35, 72, 177, 161, 152, 162, 36, 56)
keep_gp <- unique(c(keep_gp_figure2, keep_gp_figure3))
keep_gp <- sort(keep_gp)
Protein_F_pm_sparse <- Protein_F_pm_sparse[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

bk <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101)
cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#4575B4", "white", "#D73027"))(100)

pdf("Protein_Programs_heatmap_GP_of_interest.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  # t(Protein_F_pm),
  t(Protein_F_pm_sparse),
  main = "Protein programs (normalized)",
  # color scale, blue negative, white zero, red positive
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk
)
dev.off()

jj <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
  # t(Protein_F_pm),
  t(Protein_F_pm_sparse),
  main = "Protein programs (normalized)",
  # color scale, blue negative, white zero, red positive
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk
)
```

Other GPs highlighted:

```{r}
other_GPs <- c(63, 49, 175, 27, 8, 80, 35, 59, 25, 32, 50, 168, 40, 16, 174, 35, 57, 170, 152, 130, 110, 79, 159, 29, 63, 161, 166, 174, 177, 184, 199)
other_GPs <- sort(unique(other_GPs))

# take those not in the earlier set of GPs of interest
other_GPs <- other_GPs[!other_GPs %in% keep_gp]

library(pheatmap)
threshold_protein <- 0.2
Protein_F_pm_sparse <- ifelse(
  abs(Protein_F_pm) >= threshold_protein,
  Protein_F_pm,
  0
)
Protein_F_pm_sparse <- Protein_F_pm_sparse[, other_GPs, drop = FALSE]
bk <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101)
cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#4575B4", "white", "#D73027"))(100)
pdf("Protein_Programs_heatmap_other.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  # t(Protein_F_pm),
  t(Protein_F_pm_sparse)[, jj$tree_col$order, drop = FALSE],
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  main = "Protein programs (normalized)",
  # color scale, blue negative, white zero, red positive
  color = cols,
  breaks = bk
)
dev.off()
```





## CITE-seq volcano plot

```{r}
protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_mat_normalized_lognorm.rds"))

# citeseq_F <- protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm
# colnames(citeseq_F) <- paste0("K", factors_considered)
## keep only those in Protein_F_pm
# citeseq_F <- citeseq_F[rownames(Protein_F_pm) , colnames(Protein_F_pm)]

citeseq_F <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_projection_OLS_lognorm.rds"))
colnames(citeseq_F) <- paste0("K", 1:ncol(citeseq_F))

# keep only those in Protein_F_pm
citeseq_F <- citeseq_F[rownames(Protein_F_pm) , colnames(Protein_F_pm)]

# filter out cells that are not measured
protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[seurat_meta$cellID[seurat_meta$cite_seq], ]

protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, rownames(Protein_F_pm)]
mean_shifted_log_expr <- apply(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm, 2, function(x) mean(x))
plot_scatterplot <- function(factor_index, n_higlight = 10) {
  f_values <- citeseq_F[, factor_index]
  
  # normliaze f_values to have max absolute value 1
  f_values <- f_values / max(abs(f_values))
  
  gene_names <- rownames(citeseq_F)
  
  df <- data.frame(
    Gene = gene_names,
    FactorValue = f_values,
    MeanLogExpr = mean_shifted_log_expr[gene_names]
  )
  
  # plot FactorValue (x) vs MeanLogExpr (y)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = FactorValue, y = MeanLogExpr))
  p <- p + geom_point(alpha = 0.5)
  p <- p + labs(
    title = colnames(citeseq_F)[factor_index],
    x = "Increase in Shifted Log Expression",
    y = "Mean Shifted Log Expression"
  ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  # highlight top n_higlight genes with highest absolute factor values
  top_genes <- df %>%
    slice_max(order_by = abs(FactorValue), n = n_higlight)
  p <- p + geom_point(data = top_genes, aes(x = FactorValue, y = MeanLogExpr), color = "skyblue")
  p <- p + geom_text_repel(
    data = top_genes,
    aes(label = Gene),
    color = "skyblue",
    box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.15,
    max.time = 1, max.iter = 5e3
  )
  return(p)
}
```

```{r}
# store all scatterplots in a list into one PDF
pdf("CiteSeq_volcano_plots.pdf", width = 6, height = 6)
for (i in 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm)) {
  p <- plot_scatterplot(i, n_higlight = 10)
  print(p)
}
dev.off()
```

## CITE-seq protein marker sets

```{r}
marker_threshold <- 0.25
gp_marker_list <- list()
gp_marker_sign_list <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm)) {
  gp_name <- colnames(Protein_F_pm)[i]
  marker_values <- Protein_F_pm[, i]
  marker_genes <- rownames(Protein_F_pm)[which(abs(marker_values) >= marker_threshold)]
  
  if (length(marker_genes) > 5) {
    top_indices <- order(-abs(marker_values[marker_genes]))[1:5]
    marker_genes <- marker_genes[top_indices]
  }
  
  gp_marker_list[[gp_name]] <- marker_genes
  
  marker_genes_pos <- marker_genes[which(marker_values[marker_genes] >= marker_threshold)]
  marker_genes_neg <- marker_genes[which(marker_values[marker_genes] <= -marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_sign_list[[gp_name]] <- list(
    pos = marker_genes_pos,
    neg = marker_genes_neg
  )
}
```


Produce a table of marker set for each GP:

```{r}
# ---- Convert gp_marker_sign_list to a LaTeX-friendly table ----
# gp_marker_sign_list: a named list like $Set_K2$pos / $Set_K2$neg
# Each row = one set; columns = Positive markers / Negative markers
marker_list_to_df <- function(gp_marker_sign_list, collapse = ", ") {
  stopifnot(is.list(gp_marker_sign_list), length(gp_marker_sign_list) > 0)

  sets <- names(gp_marker_sign_list)
  if (is.null(sets)) stop("gp_marker_sign_list must have names, e.g. 'Set_K68'.")

  df <- data.frame(
    Set = sets,
    Positive = vapply(gp_marker_sign_list, function(x) {
      pos <- x$pos
      if (length(pos) == 0) return("")
      paste(pos, collapse = collapse)
    }, character(1)),
    Negative = vapply(gp_marker_sign_list, function(x) {
      neg <- x$neg
      if (length(neg) == 0) return("")
      paste(neg, collapse = collapse)
    }, character(1)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  df
}

# Build the table data
df_markers <- marker_list_to_df(gp_marker_sign_list)

# Optional: sort by set index (K2, K5, K6, ...)
df_markers$K_idx <- as.integer(sub(".*_K", "", df_markers$Set))
df_markers <- df_markers[order(df_markers$K_idx), c("Set", "Positive", "Negative")]

# ---- Output as LaTeX ----
# Option A: knitr::kable (simple and works well)
library(knitr)
df_markers <- df_markers[, c("Positive", "Negative")]
# If you use booktabs in your LaTeX preamble, set booktabs=TRUE
latex_code <- knitr::kable(
  df_markers,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  longtable = TRUE,     # good if the table is long
  escape = TRUE,        # keep TRUE unless you intentionally add LaTeX in cells
  caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set."
)

cat(latex_code)
```

```{r}
library(kableExtra)
tab <- knitr::kable(df_markers, format = "html", caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

html_file <- "tmp_table/table_preview.html"
png_file  <- "tmp_table/table_preview.png"
dir.create("tmp_table", showWarnings = FALSE)
kableExtra::save_kable(tab, file = html_file)
webshot2::webshot(html_file, file = png_file, zoom = 2)
png_file
```

## CITE-seq protein marker sets after clustering programs

```{r}
cor_protein_programs <- cor(Protein_F_pm, method = "pearson")

ph <- pheatmap(
  cor_protein_programs,
  main = "Correlation among protein programs"
)

hc_programs <- ph$tree_col
prog_clusters <- cutree(hc_programs, h = 2)
table(prog_clusters)
```

```{r}
gp_marker_sign_list_clustered <- list()
gp_marker_list_clustered <- list()
included_progs <- list()
for (clust_id in unique(prog_clusters)) {
  prog_in_clust <- names(prog_clusters)[prog_clusters == clust_id]
  avg_profile <- rowMeans(Protein_F_pm[, prog_in_clust, drop = FALSE])
  included_progs[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- prog_in_clust
  marker_genes <- names(avg_profile)[which(abs(avg_profile) >= marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_list_clustered[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- marker_genes
  
  marker_genes_pos <- marker_genes[which(avg_profile[marker_genes] >= marker_threshold)]
  marker_genes_neg <- marker_genes[which(avg_profile[marker_genes] <= -marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_sign_list_clustered[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- list(
    pos = marker_genes_pos,
    neg = marker_genes_neg
  )
}
```

Redo the table for clustered set:

```{r}
# Build the table data
df_markers_clustered <- marker_list_to_df(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered)
df_markers_clustered <- df_markers_clustered[order(df_markers_clustered$Set), c("Set", "Positive", "Negative")]
df_markers_clustered <- df_markers_clustered[, c("Positive", "Negative")]

# include what program each cluster corresponds to
df_markers_clustered$programs <- vapply(rownames(df_markers_clustered), function(clust_name) {
  progs <- included_progs[[clust_name]]
  paste(progs, collapse = ", ")
}, character(1))
```

```{r}
library(kableExtra)
tab2 <- knitr::kable(df_markers_clustered, format = "html", caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

html_file <- "tmp_table/table_preview_clust.html"
png_file  <- "tmp_table/table_preview_clust.png"
dir.create("tmp_table", showWarnings = FALSE)
kableExtra::save_kable(tab2, file = html_file)
webshot2::webshot(html_file, file = png_file, zoom = 2)
png_file
```



## UMAP examination of protein-activation

```{r}
MyFeatureScatter_df <- function(
  x,
  y,
  highlight,
  split = NULL,
  feature1 = "feature1",
  feature2 = "feature2",
  raster = TRUE,
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6)),
  highlight_size = 1,
  highlight_alpha = 1,
  base_pixels = c(512, 512),
  highlight_pixels = c(216, 216),
  nbin = 500
) {
  requireNamespace("scattermore", quietly = TRUE)
  requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)

  # ---- checks ----
  if (missing(x) || missing(y) || missing(highlight)) {
    stop("Please provide x, y, and highlight.")
  }
  if (length(x) != length(y) || length(x) != length(highlight)) {
    stop("x, y, and highlight must have the same length.")
  }
  if (!is.null(split) && length(split) != length(x)) {
    stop("split must have the same length as x/y.")
  }

  # coerce highlight to logical safely
  highlight <- as.logical(highlight)

  df <- data.frame(
    feature1 = as.numeric(x),
    feature2 = as.numeric(y),
    highlight = highlight,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  if (!is.null(split)) df$split <- split

  # drop rows with NA in x/y (and split if present)
  keep <- !is.na(df$feature1) & !is.na(df$feature2)
  if (!is.null(split)) keep <- keep & !is.na(df$split)
  df <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]

  df2 <- df[!is.na(df$highlight) & df$highlight, , drop = FALSE]

  # If nothing to highlight, still return the base plot
  p1 <- ggplot2::ggplot(df) +
    scattermore::geom_scattermore(
      ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2),
      color = "grey",
      pixels = base_pixels
    )

  if (nrow(df2) > 0) {
    df2$density_col <- grDevices::densCols(
      df2$feature1, df2$feature2,
      colramp = grDevices::colorRampPalette(cols),
      nbin = nbin
    )

    if (isTRUE(raster)) {
      p2 <- scattermore::geom_scattermore(
        data = df2,
        ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2, color = density_col),
        pointsize = highlight_size,
        pixels = highlight_pixels
      )
    } else {
      p2 <- ggplot2::geom_point(
        data = df2,
        ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2, color = density_col),
        size = highlight_size,
        alpha = highlight_alpha
      )
    }
  } else {
    p2 <- NULL
  }

  p <- p1 + p2 +
    ggplot2::xlab(feature1) + ggplot2::ylab(feature2) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_identity() +
    ggplot2::theme_bw() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15),
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 20),
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )

  if (!is.null(split)) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::facet_wrap(~split)
  }

  return(p)
}

MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df <- function(
  emb,                     # n x 2 matrix/data.frame: columns are dim1, dim2
  highlight,               # length n logical (or coercible): which cells to highlight
  split = NULL,            # optional length n vector for facet
  dim_names = c("Dim1", "Dim2"),
  raster = TRUE,
  highlight_size = 0.5,
  highlight_alpha = 0.5,
  base_pixels = c(512, 512),
  highlight_pixels = c(512, 512),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6)),
  nbin = 500
) {
  requireNamespace("scattermore", quietly = TRUE)
  requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)

  # ---- checks ----
  if (missing(emb) || missing(highlight)) {
    stop("Please provide emb (n x 2) and highlight.")
  }

  emb <- as.data.frame(emb)
  if (ncol(emb) < 2) stop("emb must have at least 2 columns (dim1, dim2).")
  emb <- emb[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]

  n <- nrow(emb)
  if (length(highlight) != n) stop("highlight must have length nrow(emb).")
  if (!is.null(split) && length(split) != n) stop("split must have length nrow(emb).")

  highlight <- as.logical(highlight)

  df <- data.frame(
    feature1 = as.numeric(emb[[1]]),
    feature2 = as.numeric(emb[[2]]),
    highlight = highlight,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  if (!is.null(split)) df$split <- split

  # drop NA coords (and split if present)
  keep <- !is.na(df$feature1) & !is.na(df$feature2)
  if (!is.null(split)) keep <- keep & !is.na(df$split)
  df <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]

  df2 <- df[!is.na(df$highlight) & df$highlight, , drop = FALSE]

  # ---- base plot (all cells in grey) ----
  p1 <- ggplot2::ggplot(df) +
    scattermore::geom_scattermore(
      ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2),
      color = "grey",
      pixels = base_pixels
    )

  # ---- highlight layer (density-colored) ----
  if (nrow(df2) > 0) {
    df2$density_col <- grDevices::densCols(
      df2$feature1, df2$feature2,
      colramp = grDevices::colorRampPalette(cols),
      nbin = nbin
    )

    if (isTRUE(raster)) {
      p2 <- scattermore::geom_scattermore(
        data = df2,
        ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2, color = density_col),
        pixels = highlight_pixels
      )
    } else {
      p2 <- ggplot2::geom_point(
        data = df2,
        ggplot2::aes(feature1, feature2, color = density_col),
        size = highlight_size,
        alpha = highlight_alpha
      )
    }
  } else {
    p2 <- NULL
  }

  p <- p1 + p2 +
    ggplot2::xlab(dim_names[1]) + ggplot2::ylab(dim_names[2]) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_identity() +
    ggplot2::theme_bw() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 15),
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 20),
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )

  if (!is.null(split)) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::facet_wrap(~split)
  }

  return(p)
}
```


```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K58"; selected_protein <- "CD8B"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

highlight_protein <- protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[,selected_protein] >= quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[,selected_protein], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)

p_k58 <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)
p_CD8B <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_protein,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)

# save p_k58
ggsave(p_k58, filename = "K58_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```

CD8A and CD8B predict K58, K6 and K3

```{r}
selected_GP <- "K58"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k58 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8A"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8B"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)
# relabel x and y
plot_protein_k58 <- plot_protein_k58 +
  xlab("CD8A expression") +
  ylab("CD8B expression") +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))

ggsave(plot_protein_k58, filename = "K58_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

selected_GP <- "K3"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k3 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8A"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8B"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
) +
  xlab("CD8A expression") +
  ylab("CD8B expression") +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))

ggsave(plot_protein_k3, filename = "K3_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

selected_GP <- "K6"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k6 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8A"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD8B"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
) +
  xlab("CD8A expression") +
  ylab("CD8B expression") +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))

ggsave(plot_protein_k6, filename = "K6_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```

FR4, GITR.CD357 predict high K68

```{r}
selected_GP <- "K68"
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= 0.3
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k68 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "FR4"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "GITR.CD357"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)

plot_protein_k68 <- plot_protein_k68 +
  xlab("FR4 expression") +
  ylab("GITR.CD357 expression") +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))

ggsave(plot_protein_k68, filename = "K68_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```


```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K68"; selected_protein <- "FR4"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

highlight_protein <- protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[,selected_protein] >= quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[,selected_protein], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)

p_k68 <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)
p_FR4 <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_protein,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)

ggsave(p_k68, filename = "K68_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
ggsave(p_FR4, filename = "FR4_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```


```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K68"; 
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

threshold_set <- 0.2
cells_pos <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)
market_set <- gp_marker_sign_list[[selected_GP]]
for (protein in market_set$pos) {
  cutoff <- quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein],
                     probs = 1 - threshold_set, na.rm = TRUE)
  cells_pos_new <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)[
    protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein] >= cutoff
  ]
  cells_pos <- intersect(cells_pos, cells_pos_new)
}
cells_final <- cells_pos

p_k68_set <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = rownames(L_pm_filtered) %in% cells_final,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)

ggsave(p_k68_set, filename = "K68_marker_set_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```

```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K58"; 
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

threshold_set <- 0.2
cells_pos <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)
market_set <- gp_marker_sign_list[[selected_GP]]
for (protein in market_set$pos) {
  cutoff <- quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein],
                     probs = 1 - threshold_set, na.rm = TRUE)
  cells_pos_new <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)[
    protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein] >= cutoff
  ]
  cells_pos <- intersect(cells_pos, cells_pos_new)
}
cells_final <- cells_pos

p_k58_set <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = rownames(L_pm_filtered) %in% cells_final,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)

ggsave(p_k58_set, filename = "K58_marker_set_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```



```{r}
selected_GP <- "K27"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[, selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k27 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "KLRG1"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD69"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))
plot_protein_k27 <- plot_protein_k27 +
  xlab("KLRG1 expression") +
  ylab("CD69 expression")
ggsave(plot_protein_k27, filename = "K27_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)


selected_GP <- "K63"
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[, selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading
highlight_GP_loading2 <- highlight_GP_loading[match(rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm), rownames(L_pm_filtered))]
plot_protein_k63 <- MyFeatureScatter_df(
  x = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "KLRG1"],
  y = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, "CD69"],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading2,
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("Highlighting cells with high loading in %s", selected_GP))
plot_protein_k63 <- plot_protein_k63 +
  xlab("KLRG1 expression") +
  ylab("CD69 expression")
ggsave(plot_protein_k63, filename = "K63_protein_scatter.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```

```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K63"; 
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

threshold_set <- 0.2
cells_pos <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)
cells_neg <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)
# market_set <- gp_marker_sign_list[[selected_GP]]
market_set <- list(
  pos = c("CD69", "ITB7"),
  neg = c("KLRG1")
)
for (protein in market_set$pos) {
  cutoff <- quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein],
                     probs = 1 - threshold_set, na.rm = TRUE)
  cells_pos_new <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)[
    protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein] >= cutoff
  ]
  cells_pos <- intersect(cells_pos, cells_pos_new)
}
for (protein in market_set$neg) {
  cutoff <- quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein],
                     probs = threshold_set, na.rm = TRUE)
  cells_neg_new <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)[
    protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein] <= cutoff
  ]
  cells_neg <- intersect(cells_neg, cells_neg_new)
}
# cells_final <- cells_pos
cells_final <- intersect(cells_pos, cells_neg)

p_k63_set <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = rownames(L_pm_filtered) %in% cells_final,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)
ggsave(p_k63_set, filename = "K63_marker_set_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)


p_k63 <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)
ggsave(p_k63, filename = "K63_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

```


```{r}
threshold <- 0.1
selected_GP <- "K27"; 
selected_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP]
threshold_loading <- quantile(selected_loading[selected_loading >= 0.1], (1 - threshold), na.rm = TRUE)
highlight_GP_loading <- L_pm_filtered[,selected_GP] >= threshold_loading

threshold_set <- 0.2
cells_pos <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)
# market_set <- gp_marker_sign_list[[selected_GP]]
market_set <- list(
  pos = c("KLRG1", "CD69", "ITB7"),
  neg = character(0)
)
for (protein in market_set$pos) {
  cutoff <- quantile(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein],
                     probs = 1 - threshold_set, na.rm = TRUE)
  cells_pos_new <- rownames(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm)[
    protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[, protein] >= cutoff
  ]
  cells_pos <- intersect(cells_pos, cells_pos_new)
}
cells_final <- cells_pos

p_k27_set <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = rownames(L_pm_filtered) %in% cells_final,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = rev(rainbow(10, end = 4/6))
)
ggsave(p_k27_set, filename = "K27_marker_set_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

p_k27 <- MyDimPlotHighlightDensity_df(
  emb = umap_result[match(rownames(L_pm_filtered), rownames(umap_result)), ],
  highlight = highlight_GP_loading,
  dim_names = c("UMAP1", "UMAP2"),
  cols = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#2c7bb6", "#ffffbf", "#d7191c"))(10)
)
ggsave(p_k27, filename = "K27_highlight.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)
```
